title: FICO releases GPU-accelerated optimization software with NVIDIA partnership
content:
FICO (FICO) released FICO Xpress 9.8, an optimization software that integrates NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries to accelerate computational problem-solving. The software features a GPU-accelerated implementation of the hybrid gradient algorithm that can deliver up to 50x speedups for large optimization problems.

The hybrid gradient algorithm targets extremely large, dense problems with tens or hundreds of millions of non-zeros in the problem matrix. NVIDIA CUDA-X provides GPU-accelerated libraries and frameworks that deliver higher performance than CPU alternatives across data processing and high-performance computing applications.

"The GPU-accelerated hybrid gradient algorithm represents an important addition to Xpress’s comprehensive suite of optimization algorithms, enabling organizations to solve large-scale problems faster," said Bill Waid, chief product and technology officer at FICO.

The GPU acceleration feature operates as a beta function in FICO Xpress 9.8 and supports any NVIDIA GPU with CUDA Compute Capability 7.5 or later. The software requires compatible hardware to achieve the reported performance improvements.

FICO Xpress 9.8 includes additional performance enhancements beyond GPU acceleration. The mixed-integer programming engine shows 14% faster overall performance compared to the previous version and 24% faster performance on models requiring at least 100 seconds. The global solver demonstrates 68% faster overall performance and 5.3x faster performance for models taking at least 100 seconds.

FICO offers a 60-day trial license for the FICO Xpress Optimization Suite. The company provides optimization software that enables businesses to deploy analytic models into applications powered by optimization engines for decision-making processes.

The announcement comes from a company press release statement detailing the software release and partnership with NVIDIA for GPU acceleration technology.

title: Munters shares fall 5% as NVIDIA’s Rubin chip challenges cooling norms
content:
Investing.com -- Munters shares dropped over 5% on Wednesday following NVIDIA’s announcement that its new Rubin chip can operate at significantly higher temperatures than traditional data center processors.

The Rubin chip’s ability to function at 42-45°C (108-113°F) represents a major shift from conventional chilled water cooling systems that typically operate at 7-12°C (45-54°F), with some modern low-energy designs reaching up to 18°C (64°F).

While warm-water cooling technology has been used for years in High-Performance Computing (HPC) and select hyperscale sites primarily for sustainability benefits and heat reuse, NVIDIA’s promotion of warm-water cooling as a standard for AI workloads at scale could accelerate industry-wide adoption if Rubin chips gain widespread market penetration.

According to Kepler, if warm water cooling becomes standard practice, demand for data center cooling will likely become more flexible, potentially increasing interest in hybrid cooling solutions - an area where Munters offers products.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Apollo leads $3.5 billion financing for xAI data center infrastructure
content:
Apollo Global Management (APO) announced that Apollo-managed funds led a $3.5 billion capital solution for Valor Compute Infrastructure L.P., a fund managed by Valor Equity Partners, to support a $5.4 billion acquisition and lease of data center compute infrastructure to an xAI subsidiary.

The financing uses a triple net lease structure and includes NVIDIA GB200 GPUs that will support compute clusters for ongoing model training and development of Grok, according to a company statement. NVIDIA invested in VCI as an anchor Limited Partner alongside Valor’s institutional investors.

"This transaction represents a hallmark, downside-protected investment for Apollo in the AI infrastructure space and underscores our role as a leading provider of flexible, asset-based capital for next-generation assets," said Apollo Partner Christopher Lahoud.

Antonio Gracias, Valor’s Founder, CEO and CIO, said VCI provides investors with quarterly cash distributions and upside through ownership of the compute assets.

Apollo estimates that global data center infrastructure will require several trillion dollars of investment over the next decade. Since 2022, Apollo-managed funds have deployed over $40 billion into next-generation infrastructure, including compute capacity, digital platforms and renewable energy.

As of September 30, 2025, Apollo had approximately $908 billion of assets under management. Valor had approximately $55 billion of assets under management as of December 31, 2025.

Latham & Watkins LLP served as legal counsel to the Apollo Funds, Proskauer Rose LLP served as legal counsel to VCI and Sullivan & Cromwell LLP served as legal counsel to xAI.

title: Warner Bros edges lower premarket; Mobileye surges
content:
Investing.com - U.S. stock futures sought direction on Wednesday, with investors shifting their focus away from geopolitical strife to upcoming economic data.

Here are some of the biggest premarket U.S. stock movers today:

title: Hyundai shares hit record high on Nvidia partnership speculation
content:
Investing.com -- Shares of Hyundai Motor surged Wednesday by as much as 13.8% to reach a record high during morning trading, significantly outpacing the benchmark KOSPI’s 1.2% gain.

The dramatic stock movement came amid market speculation about a potential deeper partnership between the South Korean automaker and tech giant Nvidia.

Investors appeared to be reacting to indications that Hyundai Motor Group’s relationship with Nvidia might expand beyond a basic chip supply arrangement. This speculation followed a meeting between Hyundai Motor Group Chair Euisun Chung and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the Consumer Electronics Show (CES).

The possible enhanced collaboration between the automotive manufacturer and the AI chip leader has sparked significant interest in Hyundai’s stock, pushing it to unprecedented levels in Wednesday’s trading session.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Hyundai Motor shares rise 15% to hit record high amid Nvidia tie-up speculation
content:
SEOUL, Jan 7 (Reuters) - Shares of Hyundai Motor rallied on Wednesday by as much as 14.9% to hit a record high in morning trade, outperforming the benchmark KOSPI’s 1.2% gain amid market speculation about a potential deeper tie-up with Nvidia.

Shin Yoon-chul, an analyst at Kiwoom Securities, said Hyundai’s announcement about the planned deployment in its factories of Atlas humanoid robots, made at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, was insufficient to fully explain the rally. He noted that robot production is planned at around 30,000 units by 2028 and that the Atlas product was showcased only as a static mock-up display.

However, he said investor expectations appeared to be rising that Hyundai Motor Group’s partnership with Nvidia could extend beyond a simple chip supply relationship, following a meeting between Hyundai Motor Group Chair Euisun Chung and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at CES.

Shin said the market seemed to be pricing in the possibility that Nvidia could deepen ties with the Hyundai Motor Group, including by making investments in Hyundai affiliates, which could leave further upside room in the shares of Hyundai Motor and its affiliates.

title: Trump on U.S.-Venezuela oil export deal; data deluge ahead - what’s moving markets
content:
Investing.com - Futures linked to major U.S. indices were muted, with analysts expecting the focus to turn to an upcoming raft of consequential economic indicators. President Donald Trump says the U.S. and Venezuela have agreed on a deal that will see Caracas export up to $2 billion in domestic crude to the U.S., just days after an audacious capture of Venezuela’s leader by American military forces. Elsewhere, Constellation Brands is set to report its latest quarterly results, as the brewer wrestles with muted demand.

1. Futures search for direction

U.S. stock futures hovered around both sides of the flatline on Wednesday, as investors weighed geopolitical turmoil and geared up for impending economic data.

By 02:47 ET (07:47 GMT), the Dow futures contract had risen by 46 points, or 0.1%, S&P 500 futures had fallen by 6 points, or 0.1%, and Nasdaq 100 futures had slipped by 50 points, or 0.2%.

Wall Street gained in the prior session, buoyed by an artificial intelligence-driven jump in semiconductor stocks.

Memory storage technology firms, such as Seagate Technology, SanDisk, and Micron, were boosted as well by comments from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at a closely-monitored tech industry conference. In a speech, Huang said the AI-darling’s next generation of chips will feature an additional layer of storage technology which will allow chatbots to provide quicker answers to longer questions.

Drugmaker Moderna also advanced by nearly 11% after analysts at BofA Global Research upgraded their price target for the company.

Oil stocks, meanwhile, retreated after catching a bid on Monday in the wake of a daring U.S. capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro over the weekend. Chevron and Exxon Mobil dropped 4.5% and 3.4%, respectively.

2. Venezuela agrees to export $2 billion in crude to U.S., Trump says

President Trump said on Tuesday that the United States and Venezuela had reached a deal that will see Caracas export up to $2 billion in domestic crude to the U.S.

Trump has previously demanded that Venezuela and its interim President Delcy Rodriguez effectively grant the U.S. and American oil companies full "access" to the country’s vast and lucrative oil industry.

Should it fail to do so, Trump has suggested that further U.S. military intervention could face Venezuela.

Writing in a social media post, Trump said Venezuela will be "turning over" 30 million to 50 million barrels of "sanctioned oil" to Washington. Millions of barrels of oil have been prevented from moving out of Venezuela since Trump slapped a blockade on the Latin American country in December.

Notably, the accord between the U.S. and Venezuela could initially force the reallocation of cargoes originally due to be sent to China, Reuters reported. Beijing has been a key buyer of Venezuelan crude over the last decade.

Brent crude futures declined by 0.9% to $60.18 a barrel by 03:16 ET, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude fell 1.2% to $56.46 a barrel.

3. ADP report, JOLTS ahead

Still, analysts at ING flagged in a note, the "shock" from the U.S. attack on Venezuela has "largely faded," with oil prices floating around levels prior to the incursion and equities extending gains.

"Unless the U.S. escalates threats on Greenland or intervenes again in Venezuela, markets should refocus on data in the second half of the week," the analysts including Frantisek Taborsky and Francesco Pesole said.

The White House has said Trump is mulling options for acquiring Greenland, including the possible use of the American military, with the president considering the territory to be vital for national security. European leaders, for their part, have roundly objected to Trump’s stance.

Despite the uncertainty over potential U.S. ambitions abroad, the focus in financial markets is anticipated to shift away from geopolitical developments to an upcoming batch of crucial U.S. economic data.

Economists expect a gauge from payrolls processor ADP on Wednesday to show that private U.S. companies added some 49,000 roles in December, reversing a dip of 32,000 in November. Separately, another metric is tipped to show that job openings, a proxy for labor demand, inched down slightly in November to 7.610 million.

The health of the labor market has been a crucial factor in recent interest rate decisions taken by the Federal Reserve. Policymakers slashed borrowing costs multiple times in 2025, prioritizing helping a weakening employment picture over signs of sticky inflation.

4. ISM U.S. services PMI due

Elsewhere, a tracker of activity in the key American services sector is also scheduled to be released.

The Institute for Supply Management’s non-manufacturing PMI is forecast to edge down marginally to 52.2 in December from 52.6 in the prior month.

In November, the services sector largely held steady, marked by tepid employment and higher prices for inputs.

Services are a critical piece of the U.S. economy, accounting for more than two-thirds of overall activity. As a result, the ISM figures could provide insight into the state of the world’s biggest economy at the end of the fourth quarter.

5. Constellation Brands to report

On the earnings calendar, investors are expected to focus on returns from spirit maker Constellation Brands.

The company is estimated to post comparable per-share earnings of $2.64 for its fiscal third quarter on comparable net sales of $2.16 billion, according to Bloomberg estimates.

Beer shipment volumes, in particular, are seen dropping 2.91%, while the quarterly depletion volume -- or the rate at which drinks are sold -- is anticipated to slide 3.96%. The beer division makes up the majority of Constellation’s total revenue.

Constellation and rivals like Molson Coors and Brown-Forman have been grappling with weaker demand for alcoholic beverages, along with elevated tariffs on aluminum cans which have put pressure on margins.

Immigration fears and wider economic uncertainty have also dented buying among Latino customers in the U.S., a major group for Constellation’s brands like Corona and Modelo.

title: Lenovo announces AI cloud ‘gigafactory’ tieup with Nvidia
content:
Investing.com-- Lenovo Group (HK:0992), the world’s largest PC maker, said on Tuesday it was collaborating with NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) to supply large-scale artificial intelligence computing infrastructure.

Lenovo debuted the AI Cloud Gigafactory program at the CES tradeshow in Las Vegas, with CEO Yuanqing Yang and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang debuting the partnership during a keynote address at the event.

The tie-up will provide cloud operators with hardware and software services to set up more computing capacity for AI programs, with Lenovo stating that cloud companies could achieve production-ready AI in weeks.

Lenovo will use Nvidia’s advanced Blackwell Ultra chip platform in its cloud processors, and will also support the company’s newly announced Vera Rubin platform, which Nvidia said will release later in 2026.

The Chinese PC maker also debuted Qira, a cross-device personal AI system, and unveiled a host of new enterprise-related agentic AI applications.

Lenovo has benefited from the AI boom over the last three years, as the PC maker’s enterprise business benefited from increased demand for data center servers and as more companies adopted generative AI technology.

PC sales at Lenovo’s core business also remained steady, as did its smartphone and other device offerings.

Still, the company flagged some headwinds in 2026 from rapidly increasing memory chip prices, having recently warned that it may be forced to hike the prices of its devices.

Lenovo shares fell 0.9% in Hong Kong trade, while the broader Hang Seng index fell nearly 1%.

title: Lenovo, Nvidia unveil AI cloud gigafactory
content: Jan 6 (Reuters) - Lenovo unveiled an artificial intelligence cloud gigafactory with NVIDIA at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas on Tuesday, while also showcasing a slew of other products, including a new AI platform.

title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Dow hit record close as tech shines
content:
Investing.com-- The S&P 500 and Dow closed at a record highs Tuesday, with the latter ending above 49,000 for first time ever as momentum in AI stocks helped offset weakness in energy stocks following strong gains for the latter a day earlier.

At 4:00 p.m. ET, the S&P 500 was up by 0.6%, while the Nasdaq 100 rose 0.7%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 1%, or 484 points, to closing record high of 49,462.08.

AI stocks liftoff after CES to boost tech; energy pares recent gains

At CES overnight, NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang announced the company’s next-generation AI platform, Vera Rubin, was in full production. Huang also announced that the company is releasing Alpamayo, an open-source AI model aimed at accelerating autonomous vehicle development.

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted that Huang spent more time than expected on Rubin at the event, which is typically consumer-focused. NVIDIA described demand for AI and Rubin as “skyrocketing."

Surging memory stocks added to last year’s significant gains, as the new AI chip architecture and continued demand are driving prices higher for storage and memory. Micron Technology Inc (NASDAQ:MU) gained 10%, SanDisk Corporation (NASDAQ:SNDK) jumped 23%, and Western Digital Corporation (NASDAQ:WDC) rose nearly 17%.

The jump in AI stocks boosted tech offsetting a stumble in energy, a day after the sector staged rally following the U.S capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.

’Fed speak’ kicks off ahead of jobs data

Several Federal Reserve members are set to offer their thoughts this week, potentially providing fresh insight into the Fed’s thinking on rate cuts ahead of Jan.30-31 meeting.

Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said the U.S. central bank is likely to need to cut interest rates by more than one percentage point in 2026, saying current policy is weighing on economic activity.

Speaking on Fox Business Network on Tuesday, Miran said it is hard to view monetary policy as neutral, arguing it remains clearly restrictive. He added that such restraint is holding the economy back and that cuts of well over 100 basis points would be warranted over the year.

The slew of Fed comes ahead of the release of the U.S. December jobs report on Friday, a key test for markets after recent data pointed to cooling labor market momentum.

"Dec NFP are likely to tick up to a stable 70k (private: 75k) print, higher than consensus expectations," BofA economists wrote in a note.

The report is expected to play an important role in shaping expectations around interest-rate cuts later in the year.

"For now, our base case remains that the Fed will not cut again under Powell," BofA said.

(Ayushman Ojha and Frank DeMatteo contributed to this report.)

title: BofA Securities reiterates Buy rating on Uber stock, maintains $119 price target
content:
Investing.com - BofA Securities has reiterated its Buy rating on Uber Inc. (NYSE:UBER) with a price target of $119.00, citing the company’s strategic positioning in the autonomous vehicle (AV) market.

BofA Securities believes Nvidia’s AV hardware and software solutions could significantly lower barriers for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to develop Level 4 autonomous vehicle capabilities, if successful. With a strong analyst consensus recommendation of 1.33 (Strong Buy) and 32 analysts revising earnings upwards for the upcoming period, Nvidia’s technological leadership appears well-recognized by market experts.

In a scenario with multiple AV manufacturers producing Level 4 vehicles, BofA Securities suggests Uber would have enhanced opportunities to both purchase and lease autonomous vehicles for its network, while also offering consumers ways to monetize their AVs through Uber’s platform.

In other recent news, Nvidia Corporation’s Chief Financial Officer, Colette Kress, revealed that the demand for Nvidia’s products has surpassed the company’s initial $500 billion revenue forecast for data center chips by the end of 2026. This announcement highlights the growing demand for Nvidia’s offerings in the tech industry. Meanwhile, xAI successfully completed its Series E funding round, raising $20 billion, which exceeded its initial target of $15 billion. Investors such as Valor Equity Partners and Fidelity Management & Research Company participated, along with strategic investments from NVIDIA and Cisco Investments.

Siemens and NVIDIA have expanded their partnership to develop AI solutions for industrial applications, aiming to build an AI-accelerated portfolio that includes design, simulation, and manufacturing. In another development, Sandisk saw a significant surge in its stock price after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang identified the memory storage chip market as an "unserved market" with considerable growth potential. Lastly, TD Cowen reiterated its Hold rating and $225 price target for SPX Technologies, despite market concerns over Nvidia’s cooling technology shift, suggesting that the market may have misunderstood Nvidia’s recent announcement.

title: Musk’s xAI raises $20 billion in upsized Series E funding round
content:
Jan 6 (Reuters) - Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAI announced Tuesday it raised $20 billion in an upsized Series E funding round, exceeding its $15 billion target as it ramps up development of new AI models and computing infrastructure.

Valor Equity Partners, StepStone Group, Fidelity Management & Research Company and Qatar Investment Authority participated in the round, xAI said.

Nvidia and Cisco Investments joined as strategic investors, and xAI said their backing would help it expand computing capacity.

When contacted by Reuters for comment by email, xAI replied with the message, ’Legacy Media Lies.’

xAI, which is training its next-generation Grok 5 model, said it will use the new money to build infrastructure, speed development and deployment of AI products, and fund research.

Investor demand for AI deals has stayed strong despite warnings of a bubble, as companies spend heavily on computing power and new models.

xAI has rapidly expanded its data-center footprint to train more advanced systems as it tries to close the gap with OpenAI’s ChatGPT and Alphabet’s Gemini.

title: Sandisk stock soars after Nvidia CEO calls memory storage "unserved market"
content:
Investing.com -- Sandisk stock surged 27% Tuesday after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang described the memory storage chip market as an "unserved market" with massive growth potential. Other memory and storage stocks also rallied, with Western Digital jumping 17%, Seagate Technology rising 14%, and Micron gaining 9%.

Speaking at the Consumer Electronics Show (CES) with analysts, Huang highlighted a significant opportunity in the storage sector. "For storage, that is a completely unserved market today," Huang said. "This market will likely be the largest storage market in the world, basically holding the working memory of the world’s AIs."

Huang emphasized that AI-related storage needs are outpacing current infrastructure capabilities. "The amount of context memory, the amount of token memory that we process, KB cache we process, is now just way too high. You’re not going to keep up with the old storage system," he noted.

Lynx Equity analyst KC Rajkumar attributed Micron’s rise to management discussions about visibility into 2027 during the CES meeting. Regarding Sandisk’s surge, Rajkumar pointed to "Asia checks from sell-side saying <50% of bit demand being satisfied, meaning NAND prices have further upside" and Huang’s introduction of "KV cache" - a new storage solution for Nvidia’s Rubin platform, where "each GPU is allocated 16GB of flash storage."

title: SPX Technologies stock holds steady as TD Cowen reiterates $225 target
content:
Investing.com - TD Cowen has reiterated its Hold rating and $225.00 price target on SPX Technologies (NYSE:SPXC) amid market concerns over Nvidia’s cooling technology shift. Nvidia, with its massive $4.56 trillion market cap and 65% revenue growth over the last twelve months, continues to drive significant changes in data center infrastructure.

The firm’s analysis suggests that the market may have misinterpreted Nvidia’s announcement about warm-water cooling during its CES keynote presentation, leading to an unwarranted sell-off in SPX Technologies stock.

TD Cowen emphasized that SPX’s cooling towers remain "essential for heat rejection in liquid cooled data center systems," indicating that the company’s products will continue to play a critical role despite changes in cooling technology preferences.

The research firm characterized the news as "at worst neutral for SPX (and likely +)" compared to the potentially negative impact on chiller manufacturers like Johnson Controls, Trane Technologies, and Carrier.

While the announcement might pressure chiller original equipment manufacturers, TD Cowen believes SPX Technologies is positioned more favorably in the evolving data center cooling landscape.

In other recent news, xAI has successfully raised $20 billion in its Series E funding round, surpassing its initial target of $15 billion. This funding round saw participation from notable investors such as Valor Equity Partners, Stepstone Group, and Fidelity Management & Research Company, among others. Strategic investors NVIDIA and Cisco Investments also contributed, supporting xAI’s growth in compute infrastructure and GPU cluster development. Meanwhile, NVIDIA has expanded its partnership with Siemens to develop AI solutions for industrial applications, focusing on creating an "Industrial AI operating system" to enhance design and operational processes.

In addition, NVIDIA’s Chief Financial Officer, Colette Kress, revealed that demand for the company’s products has exceeded a $500 billion revenue forecast for data center chips by the end of 2026. UBS has maintained its Buy rating on NVIDIA, citing strong demand and potential upward revisions in earnings per share for the coming years. Piper Sandler also reiterated an Overweight rating for NVIDIA, highlighting the company’s technological capabilities and expected value delivery throughout the year. These developments indicate significant investor interest and strategic advancements in the AI sector.

title: xAI secures $20 billion in Series E funding, exceeding target
content:
Investing.com -- xAI has completed its Series E funding round, raising $20 billion and exceeding its initial target of $15 billion.

The artificial intelligence company attracted investments from Valor Equity Partners, Stepstone Group, Fidelity Management & Research Company, Qatar Investment Authority, MGX, and Baron Capital Group. Strategic investors NVIDIA and Cisco Investments also participated, continuing their support for xAI’s compute infrastructure expansion and development of GPU clusters.

The funding comes after a year of significant development for xAI in 2025. The company expanded its data centers, Colossus I and II, which now contain over one million H100 GPU equivalents. xAI also advanced its Grok 4 Series frontier language models and launched Grok Voice, which offers real-time conversations in multiple languages.

The company reported approximately 600 million monthly active users across its ? and Grok applications. Other key initiatives included Grok Imagine for image and video generation and Grok on ?, which utilizes the platform for real-time information processing.

xAI is currently training Grok 5 and plans to use the new financing to accelerate infrastructure development, deploy AI products to billions of users, and advance research aligned with its mission of "Understanding the Universe."

title: Data center cooling-related stocks drop after Nvidia CEO Huang’s comments
content:
Jan 6 (Reuters) - Shares of U.S. heating, ventilation and air conditioning-related companies dropped on Tuesday after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that the company’s upcoming chips could significantly reduce cooling requirements in data centers.

Johnson Controls International dropped 7.5% to $112.40 and Trane Technologies lost 5.3% to $370.40. Both stocks hit multi-month lows and were among the biggest percentage decliners on the S&P 500.

Speaking at the Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas on Monday, Huang said Nvidia’s next generation of chips is in "full production." The Vera Rubin platform, made up of six separate Nvidia chips, is expected to debut later this year.

When talking about the cooling requirements, Huang said that "no water chillers are necessary for data centres."

Barclays analysts said in a note led by Julian Mitchell that "given the primacy of Nvidia to the whole AI ecosystem, one should not take their comments lightly, although they seem rather dramatic at first glance."

The analysts said the companies that could be most affected include Johnson Controls International, Trane Technologies and Carrier Global.

They estimate that data centers represent a low-double-digit percentage of total sales for Johnson Controls and data centers account for about 10% and 5% of sales for Trane Technologies and Carrier Global, respectively.

Carrier Global shares fell 1.1%.

The analysts also highlighted nVent Electric could benefit from these developments as it has "no presence in space cooling/chillers/air handling" but has a "decent position in data center liquid cooling."

Data center power equipment maker Vertiv Holdings could also benefit due to its "strong historical position in precision air cooling" and "strong position in liquid cooling," the analysts said.

Vertiv shares were down 2.1%, while nVent shares edged 0.5% higher.

title: US government is ’working feverishly’ on Nvidia licenses for China, ship date still unknown
content:
LAS VEGAS, Nevada, Jan 6 (Reuters) - The United States government is "working feverishly" on license applications for Nvidia to ship its H200 chips to China, but the company still does not know when they will be approved, Nvidia’s chief financial officer said on Tuesday.

In an interview with a JP Morgan analyst at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, Nevada, Nvidia CFO Colette Kress said that the company is seeing strong demand for its H200 chips from China after President Donald Trump reversed a longstanding ban on sending the chips to China last year.

title: Siemens and NVIDIA expand partnership to develop industrial AI solutions
content:
The collaboration includes plans to build what the companies describe as the world’s first fully AI-driven adaptive manufacturing sites, beginning with Siemens’ Electronics Factory in Erlangen, Germany in 2026, which will serve as a blueprint for future implementations.

"By combining NVIDIA’s leadership in accelerated computing and AI platforms with Siemens’ leading hardware, software, industrial AI and data, we’re empowering customers to develop products faster," said Roland Busch, President and CEO of Siemens AG, in a press release statement.

The partnership will also focus on accelerating semiconductor design by integrating NVIDIA’s CUDA-X libraries and GPU acceleration across Siemens’ electronic design automation portfolio, targeting 2-10x speed improvements in key workflows.

Additionally, the companies plan to jointly develop a blueprint for next-generation AI factories that balance high-density computing demands with power, cooling and automation requirements.

Several companies are already evaluating some of the capabilities developed through this partnership, including Foxconn, HD Hyundai, KION Group, and PepsiCo, according to the announcement.

In fiscal 2025, Siemens generated revenue of €78.9 billion and employed around 318,000 people worldwide as of September 30, 2025.

In other recent news, Nvidia Corporation’s Chief Financial Officer, Colette Kress, announced that demand for the company’s products has surpassed the $500 billion revenue forecast for data center chips, expected by the end of 2026. This announcement was made during a JPMorgan event, where Kress highlighted the growing demand since the initial projection. In a separate development, UBS has maintained its Buy rating on Nvidia, citing strong demand and the ramp-up of the Vera Rubin platform as key factors. Piper Sandler also reiterated an Overweight rating, emphasizing Nvidia’s technological positioning to deliver value throughout the year. KeyBanc Capital Markets echoed this sentiment, reiterating an Overweight rating and noting that the Vera Rubin platform is in full production, with significant performance improvements expected. Additionally, TF International Securities analyst Ming-Chi Kuo reported that Nvidia has renamed its AI server to VR200 NVL72, offering it in two power profiles, both surpassing previous models in power capabilities. These developments underscore Nvidia’s robust market position and technological advancements.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Siemens and NVIDIA expand partnership to develop industrial AI solutions
content:
Siemens AG and NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) announced an expansion of their strategic partnership to develop artificial intelligence solutions for industrial applications, according to a press release statement.

The companies plan to create what they term an "Industrial AI operating system" aimed at integrating AI across design, engineering, manufacturing, production, operations, and supply chain processes. NVIDIA will provide AI infrastructure, simulation libraries, models, frameworks and blueprints, while Siemens will contribute industrial AI experts and hardware and software resources.

"Together, we are building the Industrial AI operating system – redefining how the physical world is designed, built, and run - to scale AI and create real-world impact," said Roland Busch, President and CEO of Siemens AG.

The partnership includes plans to develop AI-accelerated manufacturing sites, beginning in 2026 with the Siemens Electronics Factory in Erlangen, Germany. The companies stated that Foxconn, HD Hyundai, KION Group, and PepsiCo are evaluating some capabilities.

Siemens will complete GPU acceleration across its simulation portfolio and expand support for NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries and AI physics models. The companies plan to integrate NVIDIA technologies across Siemens’ electronic design automation portfolio, targeting speed improvements in semiconductor design workflows.

The expanded partnership also includes joint development of blueprints for AI factories, combining NVIDIA’s AI platform capabilities with Siemens’ expertise in power infrastructure, electrification, and automation systems.

Both companies plan to implement the technologies in their own operations before scaling them to customers. Siemens reported revenue of €78.9 billion and net income of €10.4 billion in fiscal 2025, which ended September 30, 2025, with approximately 318,000 employees worldwide.

title: Nvidia CFO says demand exceeds $500 billion forecast
content:
Investing.com -- Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) Chief Financial Officer Colette Kress stated that demand for the company’s products continues to increase, surpassing the $500 billion revenue forecast for current and future data center chips by the end of 2026.

Speaking at a JPMorgan event on Tuesday, Kress indicated that demand has grown since the company’s initial $500 billion projection. "The $500 billion has definitely gotten larger," she said.

Regarding business in China, Kress noted that Nvidia does not yet know when or if licenses for operations in China will be approved. She added that the U.S. government is "working hard" on the approval process for these licenses.

Nvidia had previously projected $500 billion in revenue from its current and future data center chips by the end of 2026.

title: Nvidia leads Polar Capital Technology Trust’s top holdings at 10%
content:
LONDON - Polar Capital Technology Trust plc has disclosed its top equity holdings and sector exposures as of December 31, 2025, revealing that semiconductor giant Nvidia represents its largest position at 10% of the portfolio.

According to the trust’s statement, the top ten holdings account for 47.1% of the portfolio, with Alphabet (7.9%), Microsoft (5.5%), Broadcom (5.3%), and TSMC (4.9%) rounding out the top five positions.

The trust maintains significant exposure to the semiconductor industry, with the "Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment" sector representing 33.7% of the portfolio. "Interactive Media & Services" follows at 12.2%, while "Technology Hardware, Storage & Peripherals" accounts for 8.4%.

title: Is Nvidia disrupting HVAC? Johnson Controls, Trane shares dip amid cooling shift
content:
Investing.com -- Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI) stock fell 10% Tuesday following concerns that Nvidia’s (NASDAQ:NVDA) new Rubin chip platform could significantly reduce cooling requirements in data centers, potentially impacting HVAC companies’ future performance.

The selloff extended to other major HVAC players, with Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT) dropping 8% and Carrier Global (NYSE:CARR) declining 5% as investors assessed the implications of Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang’s comments at CES 2026.

During his presentation, Huang highlighted that the new Vera Rubin platform of chips will have the same cooling needs as the Grace Blackwell chip despite substantial power increases, stating that "no water chillers are necessary for datacenters" due to liquid cooling innovations.

Barclays analyst Julian Mitchell noted that these developments could particularly affect companies focused on space cooling, chillers, and air handling with minimal presence in liquid cooling. Johnson Controls, with data centers representing a low-double-digit percentage of total sales, appears most exposed among major HVAC providers.

The analyst identified Trane Technologies, with approximately 10% of sales from data centers, and Carrier Global, with roughly 5% exposure, as other companies that could face headwinds from reduced chiller intensity in future data center designs.

The news wasn’t negative for all companies in the sector. Mitchell noted that nVent Electric (NYSE:NVT) could benefit from these developments as it has "no presence in space cooling/chillers/air handling, but [does] have a decent position in datacenter liquid cooling." Vertiv Holdings (NYSE:VRT) was also identified as potentially benefiting due to its "strong historical position in precision air cooling" and "strong position in liquid cooling."

title: CES 2026 recap: Morgan Stanley flags ’skyrocketing’ AI demand
content:
Investing.com -- Nvidia’s new Rubin platform was said to be the standout theme at CES, with Morgan Stanley saying the company delivered a “decidedly positive” keynote that underscored strengthening momentum across AI hardware, automotive and robotics.

Analyst Joseph Moore wrote that CEO Jensen Huang devoted more time to Rubin than expected at what is typically a consumer-focused event.

Morgan Stanley said “Rubin will again raise the bar for performance,” noting that Nvidia emphasised system-level advances spanning GPU, CPU, networking and software.

Huang also highlighted new BlueField DPUs and Rubin CPX offerings, while describing a new inference memory platform that could “improve TPS up to 5x vs traditional storage.”

Manufacturability was another focus, with Morgan Stanley citing Nvidia’s comments that Rubin is already in “full production,” and that assembly time has been cut to “~5 minutes versus ~2 hours for Blackwell.”

The firm stated that the first rack is already being deployed, with meaningful revenue expected in the second half of 2026.

Moore added that Nvidia described demand for AI and Rubin as “skyrocketing,” and noted that management showed “no hedging on supply or demand,” despite memory remaining a key constraint.

Nvidia is also said to have reiterated that it continues to see demand from China, though licensing for the H200 remains in process.

Beyond data centre systems, Morgan Stanley pointed to Nvidia’s automotive announcements, including a new “reasoning-enabled” autonomous driving model and the upcoming launch of its Mercedes-Benz partnership.

Meanwhile, for AMD, the bank said there was “nothing new in terms of MI450, but testimonials speak to a growing ecosystem around AMD product.”

“We didn’t hear much that alters the debate around AMD stock,” Moore wrote. “The company is maintaining their conviction in MI455 as a leadership product, and with OpenAI’s support as the anchor customer we expect to see a strong ramp in Q3/Q4 of this year.

“That being said we view AMD’s success as partially a function of still overwhelming demand for compute overall vs an emerging TCO advantage vs the competition, something that will have to change to see sustained success as Nvidia continues to push the envelope across the entire technology stack.”

title: Supermicro launches AI workstations for desktop and edge computing markets
content:
Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI) announced new AI-focused computer systems targeting developers, startups, and educational institutions at a Las Vegas showcase.

The company introduced its Super AI Station (ARS-511GD-NB-LCC), featuring NVIDIA’s GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Desktop superchip. The liquid-cooled system provides 775GB of memory and delivers what Supermicro describes as more than 5x AI PFLOPS computing power compared to traditional PCIe-based GPU workstations.

Additional products include the SYS-542T-2R workstation powered by Intel Xeon 6 SoC processors, supporting various GPUs and featuring 2x 100GbE QSFP28 connectivity for applications including software-defined broadcast and video streaming services.

The company also unveiled an AI PC (AS-C521D-11302U) with AMD processors designed for office and personal use in a compact form factor. Three new edge AI systems based on AMD EPYC 4005 processors will be available in 1U, mini-1U compact, and slim tower configurations, supporting up to 16 cores.

A fanless compact edge system (SYS-E103-14P-H) features Intel Core Ultra Series 3 processors with built-in GPU and NPU capabilities, delivering up to 180 platform TOPS performance for robotics and edge AI applications.

"The consumer technology landscape is evolving faster than ever, driven by breakthroughs in consumer-focused AI applications," said Charles Liang, president and CEO of Supermicro.

The products incorporate technologies from NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD, targeting applications in higher education, research labs, and enterprise edge deployments where organizations may face limitations accessing traditional server infrastructure or cloud AI services.

title: NVIDIA selects Hesai as lidar partner for autonomous vehicle platform
content:
Hesai Technology (HSAI) announced that NVIDIA has selected the company as a lidar partner for NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Hyperion 10, a reference compute and sensor architecture designed for level 4 autonomous vehicles.

The DRIVE Hyperion platform enables automakers and developers to build autonomous vehicle fleets. Hesai joins other sensor partners that have qualified their technology on the platform, which includes cameras, radar, lidar and ultrasonic technologies.

The DRIVE Hyperion system features two NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor systems-on-a-chip built on the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture. The platform delivers more than 2,000 FP4 teraflops of real-time compute to process data from a 360-degree sensor array.

"Everything that moves will eventually become autonomous, and DRIVE Hyperion is the backbone that makes that transition possible," said Ali Kani, vice president of automotive at NVIDIA. "By unifying compute, sensors and safety into one open platform, we’re enabling our entire ecosystem, from automakers to AV SW ecosystem, to bring full autonomy to market faster."

Hesai’s ETX lidar system is designed for L3 and L4 autonomous driving applications. The product uses the company’s fourth-generation technology platform and offers long-range detection capability in a form factor designed for roof or windshield integration.

The partnership builds on a collaboration between Hesai and NVIDIA that began in 2019. The information comes from a company press release statement.

title: Visteon launches AI compute module with NVIDIA for smart cockpits and ADAS
content:
Visteon Corporation (VC) announced the launch of its AI-ADAS Compute Module powered by NVIDIA technology. The module enables automakers to deploy either intelligent cockpit experiences or advanced driver assistance systems using a single platform.

The product functions as a plug-and-play solution that automakers can configure for specific needs without overhauling existing electrical systems. The module can power AI-driven cockpit features such as voice assistants and personalized experiences, or enable ADAS safety functions.

The AI-ADAS Compute Module uses NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin system-on-a-chip and runs on the NVIDIA DriveOS operating system. For in-cabin AI applications, Visteon developed a hybrid edge-cloud architecture using NVIDIA AI Enterprise software and NVIDIA NIM microservices.

"Automakers need flexible, production-ready solutions that can integrate into their current vehicle platforms while preparing them for a software-defined future," said Sivakumar Yeddanapudi, Global Vice President of Digital Cockpit and Connected Services at Visteon.

When configured for ADAS applications, the module serves as a compute foundation that suppliers and automakers can build upon with their own advanced driver assistance software.

The system includes multimodal AI capabilities that combine data from cameras, infotainment systems, vehicle sensors, and voice to deliver contextual experiences. Visteon’s cognitoAI software, developed for NVIDIA AI infrastructure, enables this multimodal intelligence.

The module is designed for integration into both legacy and new vehicle architectures with standardized input/output features. The company states the solution aims to reduce engineering risk and time-to-market for automakers.

Visteon, headquartered in Van Buren Township, Michigan, recorded annual sales of approximately $3.87 billion in 2024 and secured $6.1 billion in new business, according to the company statement.

title: Nvidia’s VR200 NVL72 AI server brings major power upgrades - Kuo
content:
Investing.com -- Nvidia has renamed its AI server from VR200 NVL144 to VR200 NVL72 and will offer it in two power profiles, according to TF International Securities analyst Ming-Chi Kuo following CEO Jensen Huang’s CES 2026 keynote on Monday.

The VR200 NVL72 will come in Max Q and Max P configurations, sharing the same hardware design but with different power profiles. Max Q will feature GPU/rack power of approximately 1.8/190 kilowatts, while Max P will offer about 2.3/230 kilowatts. Both configurations significantly exceed the GB300 NVL72’s 1.4/140 kilowatts.

Kuo noted that offering dual power profiles improves deployment flexibility under data center power constraints, indicating Nvidia is increasingly considering real-world infrastructure limitations in its product specifications.

The new server features upgraded thermal solutions, with both configurations using a micro-channel cold plate paired with a gold-plated lid. The analyst mentioned that while the market expected a transition to micro-channel lid technology at the 2.3 kW threshold, such production is unlikely before the second half of 2027.

The VR200 NVL72 delivers approximately 3.5 times the training power and 5 times the inference AI computing power compared to the GB300 NVL72. This performance boost requires substantial power infrastructure upgrades, including new power shelves rated at 33U 110 kW with a 3+1 redundancy design and increased power whip ratings of 100A, up from 60A.

Liquid cooling plays an even more critical role in the new server, with both compute and NVSwitch trays adopting a fanless design. The rack technology cooling system flow nearly doubles compared to the previous generation, while airflow requirements drop by roughly 80%.

A key innovation highlighted by Huang during his keynote is the compute tray’s new midplane design, enabling a cableless configuration that reportedly reduces assembly time from about two hours to just five minutes.

Production timelines indicate Rubin CoWoS wafers will reach 300-350k in 2026, with pilot production starting in early Q1 and mass production by late Q2. VR200 NVL72 rack assembly is expected to enter mass production by the end of Q3 2026, with estimated shipments of 5,000-7,000 units in the second half of 2026.

Kuo suggested the VR200 NVL72 likely represents the final generation of Nvidia’s Oberon rack architecture, as power requirements approaching 200 kW create constraints for 54V distribution. The company is expected to transition to its next-generation Kyber rack design supporting 800V HVDC for future products.

title: Morning Bid: Swiftly moving on
content:
By Marc Jones

LONDON, Jan 6 (Reuters) - What matters in U.S. and global markets today

Global stocks’ march to ever-higher peaks undefined is still going strong, although both Wall Street futures and the dollar look a tad groggy this morning. Perhaps there’s a bit of a delayed hangover kicking in this morning after all the new year’s excitement.

I’ll get into all the market news below.

But first check out the latest episode of the Morning Bid podcast and remember to subscribe to hear top Reuters journalists discuss the biggest news in markets and finance seven days a week.

Today’s Market Minute * Toppled Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro pleaded notguilty on Monday to narcotics charges after President DonaldTrump’s stunning capture of him rattled world leaders and leftofficials in Caracas scrambling to regroup. * Geopolitics, U.S. midterm elections and diverging monetarypolicies are likely to be among the key drivers for worldmarkets in 2026, alongside an artificial intelligence boom thathas raised concerns about a tech share bubble. * An unknown trader has raked in a profit worth about$410,000 after betting that Venezuelan president Nicolas Madurowould be ousted from his position. * Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said on Monday that the company’snext generation of chips is in “full production,” saying theycan deliver five times the AI computing of the company’sprevious chips when serving up chatbots and other AI apps. * U.S. President Donald Trump is giving U.S. energycompanies the opportunity to revive Venezuela’s massive,derelict oil industry. It’s an offer they may want to refuse,writes ROI Energy Columnist Ron Bousso.

SWIFTLY MOVING ON

Wall Street is expecting a nice quiet start. There is little on the U.S. data calendar other than the normally non-market moving ’final’ PMIs for December, although traders only have to wait until Friday for the first nonfarm payrolls of the year and whether they need to adjust their thinking on how many interest rate cuts the Federal Reserve might deliver this year.

Otherwise, markets will continue to watch Donald Trump’s evolving plans to “run” Venezuela - and try and gauge just how much that could end up costing (or earning) the U.S.

A little over 72 hours since the removal of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, few marks have been left on the currency market. Monday’s initial bolt for dollar safety has proved very short-lived, as signs of dialogue between the White House and Maduro’s successor, Delcy Rodríguez, suggest further U.S. military action is unlikely for now.

December ISM manufacturing index data also played a role. It dropped below 48 on Monday, a fourth consecutive monthly decline and to the lowest level since October 2024. The backlog of orders also continued to shrink, suggesting a risk of inventory build-up and a potential hit to employment in the months ahead - something Fed official Neel Kashkari also happened to point out.

Back to today and oil is ticking up again too. All the talk of a glut of Venezuelan crude driving down prices looks to have been clipped by the reality that it will take years - and billions of dollars - that the U.S. oil majors might not jump at forking out. Gold and silver have continued to nudge up, however, as has Wall Street’s so-called fear gauge the VIX, although that really is from rock-bottom levels.

As for Venezuela’s Maduro, his situation appears pretty clear. He is expected to be convicted by the Southern District of New York on at least one of the four charges - narco-terrorism, cocaine importation conspiracy and possession of machine guns and destructive devices - levied against him, and will likely spend the rest of his life in jail.

Chart of the day

One of the things that has been most striking about Maduro’s shock weekend capture is that barring Venezuela’s default-stricken government bonds and some of the share prices of the oil majors, it barely seems to have registered for broader global markets.

The dollar, which would normally expect a safe-haven bid, is back on the ropes this morning, crude prices are almost exactly where they were this time last week and with MSCI’s main world stocks benchmark already at its second record high of the year, volatility is threatening to go extinct.

Today’s events to watch

Final S&P U.S. PMIs for December

Richmond Federal Reserve President Thomas Barkin speaks

U.S. Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran speaks

title: Samsung likely to flag 160% jump in Q4 profit as AI boom stokes chip prices
content:
By Hyunjoo Jin

SEOUL, Jan 6 (Reuters) - Samsung Electronics is expected to flag a 160% jump in its fourth-quarter operating profit spurred by a severe chip shortage that has sharply driven up memory prices as customers scramble to meet booming demand for artificial intelligence.

Semiconductor prices have rocketed in recent months, as the industry’s shift to AI-related chips has curbed production for traditional memory, while demand has been surging for both conventional and advanced chips to train and run AI models.

Samsung is likely to estimate an operating profit of 16.9 trillion won ($11.7 billion) for the October to December period, according to LSEG SmartEstimate from 31 analysts, which is weighted toward those who are more consistently accurate.

This compares with 6.49 trillion won from a year earlier and would mark the highest quarterly profit since the third quarter of 2018, which was a record high of 17.6 trillion won.

Some analysts have in recent weeks raised their estimates for Samsung’s fourth-quarter operating profit to more than 20 trillion won on the back of stronger-than-expected prices of traditional chips.

The world’s top memory chip maker is set to release its estimates for revenue and operating profit on Thursday.

SOARING MEMORY CHIP PRICES

Prices for a type of DDR5 DRAM chip jumped 314% in the fourth quarter from a year earlier, according to data from market researcher TrendForce.

It expects conventional DRAM contract prices to rise 55% to 60% in the current quarter, from the October to December period.

"As conventional DRAM prices continue to surge, Samsung - whose production capacity is largely concentrated in this segment - stands to gain relatively more from the current price upcycle," TrendForce analyst Avril Wu said.

DRAM chips are used in servers, computers and smartphones to temporarily store data and help run programs and applications smoothly and swiftly. DDR5 DRAM, a conventional chip, is faster and more efficient than its predecessor.

In December, Micron Technology forecast second-quarter adjusted profit at nearly double Wall Street estimates. Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said he expects memory markets to remain tight past 2026 and that in the medium term, it expects to meet only half to two-thirds of demand from several key customers.

NVIDIA, HBM, MOBILE

Samsung’s booming profit marks a dramatic turnaround, just over a year after CEO Jun Young-hyun apologised for the company’s disappointing earnings and performance as it lagged its cross-town rival SK Hynix in supplying high-end chips to Nvidia, the dominant maker of AI processors.

Samsung shares jumped 125% last year, marking their biggest annual percentage gain in 26 years. They fell 2.1% on Tuesday morning in a wider market that was down 0.2%, taking a breather after their recent rally.

On Friday, Jun said Samsung customers have praised the competitive edge of its next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, or HBM4, quoting them as saying, "Samsung is back," which helped extend a rally in Samsung’s shares to record highs in recent sessions.

He did not elaborate on those customers, but analysts said Samsung is making progress supplying chips to Nvidia, potentially gaining share against SK Hynix and Micron.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said on Monday the company’s next generation of chips is in full production. The Vera Rubin platform, which will incorporate HBM4 chips, is on track to arrive later this year, Nvidia said.

Analysts said Samsung’s operating profit is expected to more than double this year to over 100 trillion won as the chip price rally would more than offset slowing profit for its mobile business.

Lee Min-hee, an analyst at BNK Investment & Securities, said he was wary of Samsung’s valuations, as surging chip prices may sap demand for PCs and smartphones, and cited "risks of a demand slowdown" for AI data centres increasingly relying on debt to finance investments.

While the global shortage of memory chips is a boon to Samsung’s mainstay semiconductor business, soaring chip prices are squeezing margins in its smartphone division, its second-largest revenue source.

"As this situation is unprecedented, no company is immune to its impact," Samsung co-CEO TM Roh, who oversees Samsung’s mobile, TV and home appliance businesses, told Reuters, adding it is trying to minimise the impact, which looks "inevitable".

($1 = 1,446.5000 won)

title: Futures flat, CES 2026 takes central stage- what’s moving markets
content:
Investing.com-- Global markets remained driven by momentum on Tuesday, with Asian equities extending their rally into the new year and setting a constructive tone for Europe.

1) Asia, Europe shares gain

Shares in Hong Kong, Japan and Taiwan led gains in Asia, building on solid advances in U.S. markets earlier. Chipmakers were at the forefront after fresh updates from Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices at the CES 2026 technology event in Las Vegas, reinforcing enthusiasm around artificial intelligence spending.

European shares followed higher, with the FTSE 100 outperforming and rising about 0.6%.

2) U.S. stocks modestly lower

U.S. equity futures, however, were modestly lower, pointing to a softer open on Wall Street. E-mini Dow futures slipped around 0.2%, while S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures edged down by about 0.1%, suggesting some near-term caution after recent record-setting moves in U.S. cash markets.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed to a fresh record Monday after the U.S. removed Venezuela’s president, Nicolás Maduro, and President Donald Trump said American energy producers would help restore the country’s oil output.

Broader gains were also supported by strength in growth stocks, with shares of Tesla, Amazon and Palantir posting sharp advances.

3) CES 2026 headlines take central stage

CES headlines continued to shape sentiment in the technology space. AMD unveiled a new chip aimed at corporate data centers as it seeks to challenge Nvidia’s dominance in AI hardware.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said demand for the company’s upcoming Rubin data center processors is very strong, noting that production is underway and customers will soon begin testing the products.

He highlighted new BlueField and Rubin CPX offerings designed for increasingly complex inference workloads, while AMD reiterated confidence in its MI455 platform, supported by OpenAI as an anchor customer, with expectations for a strong ramp later this year.

4) Precious metal rally continues

In commodities, metals pushed higher again. Silver jumped more than 2%, copper rose 1.5% to a fresh record, and gold gained 0.6%, moving closer to $4,500 an ounce. Concerns that the Trump administration could introduce tariffs on refined metals have drawn copper into the U.S., raising fears of shortages elsewhere.

Oil prices edged down about 0.4% after President Donald Trump said his administration may subsidize oil investment in Venezuela, though details remain scarce amid signs of renewed political tension in the country.

5) Geopolitics stay in focus

Denmark warned that any U.S. attack on Greenland would effectively end NATO, pushing back against Trump’s remarks about taking control of the territory.

Separately, U.S. negotiators are set to meet European leaders in Paris as discussions continue over postwar security guarantees for Ukraine.

title: Nebius to deploy NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 platform in H2 2026
content:
Nebius Group (NBIS) announced it will deploy NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin NVL72 accelerated computing platform through its AI Cloud and Token Factory services starting in the second half of 2026. The Amsterdam-based company will integrate the platform across its data center infrastructure in the United States and Europe.

The NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 platform was launched at CES 2026 and is designed for complex AI workloads including agentic applications, advanced reasoning, and large-scale mixture-of-experts models. Nebius stated it will be among the first AI cloud providers to offer the new platform to customers.

"We are proud to be one of the first on the market to offer Vera Rubin GPUs as we fuel the next wave of AI innovation," said Arkady Volozh, founder and CEO of Nebius. "By integrating Vera Rubin into Nebius AI Cloud and our inference platform Nebius Token Factory, we’re giving AI innovators and enterprises the infrastructure they need to develop agentic and reasoning AI systems faster and more efficiently."

The Rubin platform will complement Nebius’s existing NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 and NVIDIA Grace Blackwell Ultra NVL72 capacity. Through Nebius AI Cloud, customers will receive direct infrastructure access, while Nebius Token Factory provides an enterprise inference and post-training platform for open-source model development.

Dave Salvator, director of accelerated computing products at NVIDIA, stated that Nebius’s AI-native infrastructure will enable customers to deploy NVIDIA Rubin-powered AI applications in production. Nebius operates as an NVIDIA Cloud Partner and holds NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud Partner status, with its infrastructure validated against NVIDIA reference architectures and benchmarks.

title: Nvidia CEO showcases AI momentum at CES 2026, William Blair reiterates Outperform
content:

During the keynote and analyst session, Huang highlighted sustained strong momentum in artificial intelligence and demonstrated Nvidia’s system-level portfolio across chips, hardware, and AI models, according to William Blair analyst notes.

While the event featured few major product announcements, the firm noted several positive developments, including a new L4 autonomous driving reasoning model called Alpamayo, a new Inference Context Memory Storage Platform designed to address memory constraints in AI performance, and additional details on the upcoming Vera Rubin system.

Nvidia shares traded roughly flat after-hours at $188.22 following the presentation, representing a price-to-earnings ratio of 27 times William Blair’s fiscal 2027 estimate, with the firm maintaining a $245 fair value target based on approximately 35 times its fiscal 2027 EPS estimate.

In other recent news, Nvidia has been the focus of several analyst reviews and strategic announcements. Raymond James reiterated its Strong Buy rating on Nvidia, maintaining a price target of $272.00. Nvidia provided clarification that its DGX Cloud strategy aims to demonstrate versatility rather than compete with customers.

In other recent news, Nvidia announced the introduction of its new Inference Context Memory Storage Platform, powered by the BlueField-4 data processor. This AI-native storage infrastructure is designed to enhance GPU memory capacity and improve the sharing of key-value cache data across AI computing nodes, potentially boosting tokens per second by up to five times. Additionally, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled the Vera Rubin AI platform, which will feature six new AI chips and a pod-level context memory storage platform, set for release in the latter half of 2026. In a related development, Hesai has been selected by Nvidia as its laser technology partner for the DRIVE AGX Hyperion 10 platform, aimed at achieving Level 4 autonomous driving capabilities. Analyst firms have also weighed in on Nvidia’s recent announcements. BofA Securities reaffirmed its Buy rating with a $275 price target, while Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform rating with a $352 target, citing Nvidia as a top pick for 2026. title: HP unites OMEN and HyperX brands, unveils new gaming laptop and monitor
content:
HP Inc. (HPQ) announced the consolidation of its OMEN and HyperX gaming brands under the HyperX name at CES 2026, along with the introduction of new gaming hardware products.

The company unveiled the HyperX OMEN MAX 16 gaming laptop, which delivers up to 300W Total Platform Power through Intel Core Ultra 200HX series processors, AMD Ryzen AI processors, and up to NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 Laptop GPU. The laptop features a redesigned cooling system with three fans and includes a high-polling rate keyboard that operates up to four times faster than previous generations.

HP also introduced the HyperX OMEN OLED 34 gaming monitor, equipped with V-stripe QD-OLED panel technology. The 34-inch display offers 21:9 WQHD resolution, 360Hz refresh rate, and 0.03ms response time. The monitor includes 100W USB-C power delivery and a built-in KVM switch.

The HyperX Clutch Tachi represents HP’s first Xbox-licensed arcade controller, featuring a leverless design with Magnetic Switches and TMR sensors. Users can customize button mapping and actuation through NGENUITY software.

HP announced a partnership with Neurable to develop a gaming headset using neurotechnology and AI to interpret brain activity. The headset aims to help players improve focus and accuracy through real-time brain activity monitoring.

"As we bring OMEN and HyperX together, we’re continuing to push the boundaries of gaming innovation," said Josephine Tan, Senior Vice President and Division President of Personal Systems Gaming Solutions at HP Inc.

All three hardware products are expected to be available on HP.com in Spring 2026, with pricing to be announced closer to launch dates.

title: AMD unveils new AI processors at CES Las Vegas, targets 1000x performance jump
content:
Investing.com-- AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) CEO Lisa Su unveiled a new line of PC and artificial intelligence processors at the CES event in Las Vegas on Monday evening, and said that the company was on track to deliver a thousandfold increase in the AI performance of its chips.

Su unveiled the AMD Ryzen AI 400 series– a new line of PC chips, and also showed off the company’s MI455 AI processors, which are a key component of the data centers that power AI programs. AMD has a deal with OpenAI to supply data center chips to the ChatGPT maker.

OpenAI President Greg Brockman joined Su on stage to tout OpenAI’s offerings and their dependence on advanced processors.

The AMD CEO showed off the MI500 processor, which she claimed offers 1,000 times the performance of an older version. The chip will be released in 2027.

Commenting on the keynote, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore said he "didn’t hear much that alters the debate around AMD stock."

He said the company remains confident in MI455 as a leadership product, with OpenAI “as the anchor customer,” and expects a strong ramp in the third and fourth quarters of this year.

However, AMD’s recent traction is driven partly by “still overwhelming demand for compute overall” rather than a clear total cost of ownership (TCO) advantage versus competitors, Moore added, something he thinks needs to change for success to be sustained as Nvidia “continues to push the envelope across the entire technology stack.”

The AMD reveal comes just hours after NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang previewed a host of advanced AI chips at CES. Huang showed off Nvidia’s next-generation Vera Rubin AI processor platform, which he said was in full production and expected to debut later in 2026.

AMD has struggled to match the stellar success of Nvidia in developing and selling the most advanced AI chips in the market. While the company has benefited from increased AI-fueled demand, it has lagged larger rival Nvidia in terms of sales.

The company signed a major deal with OpenAI in October, which stands to bring in hundreds of billions in revenue if successfully executed. AMD will supply key processors for OpenAI’s data centers.

(Ambar Warrick contributed to this report.)

title: AMD shows off new higher performing AI chip at CES event
content:
By Max A. Cherney and Gnaneshwar Rajan

Jan 5 (Reuters) - Advanced Micro Devices CEO Lisa Su showed off a number of the company’s AI chips on Monday at the CES trade show in Las Vegas, including its advanced MI455 AI processors, which are components in the data center server racks that the company is selling to firms like ChatGPT maker OpenAI.

Su also unveiled the MI440X, a version of the MI400 series chip designed for on-premise use at businesses. The so-called enterprise version is designed to fit into infrastructure that is not specifically designed for AI clusters. The MI440X is a version of an earlier chip that the U.S. plans to use in a supercomputer.

AMD is one of Nvidia’s strongest rivals but has struggled to have as much success. In October, AMD signed a deal with OpenAI that, in addition to the financial upside, was a major vote of confidence in AMD’s AI chips and software. But it is unlikely to dent Nvidia’s dominance, as the market leader continues to sell every AI chip it can make, analysts said.

At the Monday event, OpenAI President Greg Brockman joined Su on stage and said chip advancements were critical to OpenAI’s vast computing needs.

Looking to the future needs of companies like OpenAI, Su previewed the MI500 and said it offered 1,000 times the performance of an older version of the processor. The company said the chips would launch in 2027.

At the event, Su hosted Daniele Pucci, CEO of Generative Bionics, an Italian AI developer, who unveiled GENE.01, a humanoid robot.

"Our first commercial humanoid robot will be manufactured in the second half of 2026," Pucci said at the event.

Earlier on Monday, Nvidia showed off its next-generation Vera Rubin platform, which is made up of six separate chips. CEO Jensen Huang said it was in full production. It is expected to debut later this year.

In October, AMD signed the deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI that will add billions of dollars to the company’s annual revenue. The first deployment of AI chips that incorporate AMD’s MI400 series will roll out this year. Nvidia has generated tens of billions of dollars in quarterly revenue from its AI chip sales, a feat that AMD has struggled to achieve thus far.

OpenAI is a key customer of AMD and executives at the Santa Clara, California-based company expect the deal to lead to significant additional new sales.

Also on Monday, AMD launched its Ryzen AI 400 Series processors for AI PCs, alongside Ryzen AI Max+ chips for advanced local inference and gaming. Intel held a launch event earlier for its Panther Lake chips that it said would be available for order on Tuesday.
