title: Nvidia CEO Huang says next generation of chips is in full production
content:
By Stephen Nellis

LAS VEGAS, Jan 5 (Reuters) - Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said on Monday that the company’s next generation of chips is in “full production,” saying they can deliver five times the artificial-intelligence computing of the company’s previous chips when serving up chatbots and other AI apps.

In a speech at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, the leader of the world’s most valuable company revealed new details about its chips, which will arrive later this year and which Nvidia executives told Reuters are already in the company’s labs being tested by AI firms, as Nvidia faces increasing competition from rivals as well as its own customers.

The Vera Rubin platform, made up of six separate Nvidia chips, is expected to debut later this year, with the flagship server containing 72 of the company’s graphics units and 36 of its new central processors. Huang showed how they can be strung together into "pods" with more than 1,000 Rubin chips and said they could improve the efficiency of generating what are known as "tokens" - the fundamental unit of AI systems - by 10 times.

To get the new performance results, however, Huang said the Rubin chips use a proprietary kind of data that the company hopes the wider industry will adopt.

"This is how we were able to deliver such a gigantic step up in performance, even though we only have 1.6 times the number of transistors," Huang said.

While Nvidia still dominates the market for training AI models, it faces far more competition - from traditional rivals such as Advanced Micro Devices as well as customers like Alphabet’s Google - in delivering the fruits of those models to hundreds of millions of users of chatbots and other technologies.

Much of Huang’s speech focused on how well the new chips would work for that task, including adding a new layer of storage technology called “context memory storage” aimed at helping chatbots provide snappier responses to long questions and conversations.

Nvidia also touted a new generation of networking switches with a new kind of connection called co-packaged optics. The technology, which is key to linking together thousands of machines into one, competes with offerings from Broadcom and Cisco Systems.

Nvidia said that CoreWeave will be among the first to have the new Vera Rubin systems and that it expects Microsoft, Oracle, Amazon and Alphabet to adopt them as well.

In other announcements, Huang highlighted new software that can help self-driving cars make decisions about which path to take - and leave a paper trail for engineers to use afterward. Nvidia showed research about software, called Alpamayo, late last year, with Huang saying on Monday it would be released more widely, along with the data used to train it so that automakers can make evaluations.

"Not only do we open-source the models, we also open-source the data that we use to train those models, because only in that way can you truly trust how the models came to be," Huang said from a stage in Las Vegas.

Last month, Nvidia scooped up talent and chip technology from startup Groq, including executives who were instrumental in helping Alphabet’s Google design its own AI chips. While Google is a major Nvidia customer, its own chips have emerged as one of Nvidia’s biggest threats as Google works closely with Meta Platforms and others to chip away at Nvidia’s AI stronghold.

During a question-and-answer session with financial analysts after his speech, Huang said the Groq deal "won’t affect our core business" but could result in new products that expand its lineup.

At the same time, Nvidia is eager to show that its latest products can outperform older chips like the H200, which U.S. President Donald Trump has allowed to flow to China. Reuters has reported that the chip, which was the predecessor to Nvidia’s current "Blackwell" chip, is in high demand in China, which has alarmed China hawks across the U.S. political spectrum.

Huang told financial analysts after his keynote that demand is strong for the H200 chips in China, and Chief Financial Officer Colette Kress said Nvidia has applied for licenses to ship the chips to China but was waiting for approvals from the U.S. and other governments to ship them.

title: Asia chipmaking stocks rise after Nvidia unveils new AI processor
content:
Investing.com-- Asian semiconductor shares climbed on Tuesday as investors cheered Nvidia’s unveiling of its latest AI processor platform at the CES 2026 trade show in Las Vegas, with optimism that demand for advanced chips will buoy global tech growth.

Nvidia’s new Vera Rubin AI computing platform, which integrates next-generation GPUs and CPUs for data centre workloads, was showcased on Monday, reinforcing the company’s leadership in artificial intelligence hardware.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) (TW:2330) led regional gains, rising 2% to a fresh record high, as it stands to benefit from strong production demand for Nvidia-designed chips.

In South Korea, Samsung Electronics (KS:005930) reversed earlier losses to trade 1% higher, while SK Hynix Inc (KS:000660) jumped more than 4%.

Across Japan’s markets, major chip-related names including Renesas Electronics Corp (TYO:6723) and Advantest Corp (TYO:6857) posted gains, with broader tech buying lifting the TOPIX index to fresh highs.

In China and Hong Kong, Hua Hong Semiconductor (HK:1347) added 1.4% while NAURA Technology Group (SZ:002371) gained 1.1%, but some semiconductor stocks were seen trimming gains on Tuesday amid China’s self-reliance bets.

A broader tech optimism around artificial intelligence continues to attract flows into Asian chipmakers, underscoring the region’s critical role in the global AI supply chain.

title: Chinese lidar maker Hesai surges on Nvidia autonomous driving tie-up
content:
Investing.com-- Shares of Chinese lidar maker Hesai Group (HK:2525) surged on Tuesday after Nvidia said the company had joined its expanding autonomous driving ecosystem, bolstering investor optimism around demand for sensors used in self-driving vehicles.

At the CES trade show in Las Vegas, Nvidia announced that Hesai is among the latest sensor partners qualifying their technology on its DRIVE Hyperion platform, a production-ready architecture designed to support level-4 autonomous driving.

The move places Hesai alongside global automotive suppliers and technology firms working with Nvidia to accelerate the commercial rollout of advanced driverless systems.

Hong Kong-listed shares of the company jumped as much as 7.6% to HK$197.10, their highest in nearly three months.

Nvidia said the expanding DRIVE Hyperion ecosystem now includes a broad range of lidar, camera, and radar suppliers, giving automakers confidence that sensing hardware is fully compatible with its end-to-end autonomous vehicle platform.

By unifying compute, sensors, and safety systems, the company aims to shorten development cycles, reduce testing costs, and speed up time to market.

Investors have increasingly focused on lidar makers as spending on autonomous driving, robotaxis, and AI-enabled vehicles gathers momentum worldwide.

title: Dow hits record, energy stocks end higher after US strikes Venezuela
content:
By Noel Randewich

Jan 5 (Reuters) - Wall Street ended higher on Monday, with surging financial shares helping lift the Dow Jones Industrial Average to an all-time peak, while energy firms jumped after a U.S. military strike captured Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.

Investors bet Washington’s move against Venezuela’s leadership would allow U.S. firms access to the world’s largest oil reserves. President Donald Trump’s administration plans to meet with executives from U.S. oil companies this week to discuss boosting Venezuelan production.

The S&P 500 energy index rose 2.7% to its highest since March 2025, with heavyweights Exxon Mobil and Chevron both surging.

Weapons manufacturers also advanced after Washington’s military action. Lockheed Martin and General Dynamics climbed, while the S&P 500 aerospace and defense index rose to a record high.

"Energy stocks are really benefiting from the expectation that President Trump is intending to send them in to do more investment in Venezuela and ultimately make more money for themselves," said Rob Haworth, senior investment strategist at U.S. Bank Wealth Management in Seattle.

"The lack of permanent boots on the ground, the fact that we’re not permanently engaged, means the broader equity markets are able to set aside what might have been fears of a prolonged engagement," Haworth said.

Tesla climbed 3.1% after seven straight sessions of losses. Nvidia dipped 0.4% and Apple declined 1.4%.

The S&P 500 climbed 0.64% to end the session at 6,902.05 points.

The Nasdaq gained 0.69% to 23,395.82 points, while the Dow rose 1.23% to 48,977.18 points. Volume on U.S. exchanges was heavy, with 19.1 billion shares traded, far exceeding the average of 15.9 billion shares over the previous 20 sessions.

The S&P 500 financials index jumped 2.2% as investors looked to upcoming quarterly reports. Analysts on average see S&P 500 financial companies growing their earnings 6.7% year-over-year in the December quarter.

Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase rose more than 3% and hit record highs.

"The mood has been favoring financial stocks in recent days and as people look beyond tech, this is a sector many are choosing to look toward," said Steve Sosnick, chief market analyst at Interactive Brokers.

Wall Street’s main indexes posted double-digit gains in 2025 for the third consecutive year, a run last seen in 2021.

Data showed U.S. manufacturing contracted more than expected in December, extending a 10-month slump.

The spotlight will now be on the monthly nonfarm payrolls on Friday, which could influence the Federal Reserve’s monetary policy in 2026.

Markets are pricing in about 60 basis points of interest rate easing this year, according to LSEG.

Cryptocurrency-linked shares advanced as bitcoin hit a more than three-week high. Strategy, formerly MicroStrategy, climbed almost 5% and Coinbase rallied 7.8%. Goldman Sachs upgraded Coinbase to "buy" from "neutral".

Advancing issues outnumbered falling ones within the S&P 500 by a 2.1-to-one ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 60 new highs and 11 new lows; the Nasdaq recorded 107 new highs and 49 new lows.

title: Intel launches next-gen PC chip at CES in Las Vegas
content:
By Max A. Cherney

Jan 5 (Reuters) - Intel launched Panther Lake, its new AI chip for laptops, on Monday at the CES trade show in Las Vegas, as the company seeks to reassure investors about the first product made using its next-generation manufacturing process called 18A.

Jim Johnson, senior vice president and general manager of Intel’s PC group, offered technical details about the company’s first line of Panther Lake chips known as Intel Core Ultra Series 3. The chips feature a new transistor design and a way to deliver power to the chip due to the company’s 18A manufacturing process.

At the event, Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan said the company made good on its promise to ship its first products with the 18A manufacturing process in 2025, referring to the Panther Lake chips.

Intel’s prior-generation Lunar Lake chips were largely made by TSMC. The stakes for Intel are high - the company is making its first high-volume product with 18A, and hopes to reclaim market share it has lost to Advanced Micro Devices.

Johnson said the company has created a separate graphics chiplet - a mini chip stitched together with other mini-chips to form a complete processor. On Monday, Intel said the Intel Core Ultra Series 3 chips would deliver 60% better performance than the prior-generation Lunar Lake Series 2.

Intel plans to launch a platform for handheld video games based on the Panther Lake designs this year, Johnson said. Handheld PCs designed by a range of suppliers have grown in popularity in recent years.

Intel has struggled with the yield, or the number of good chips per silicon wafer, for the Panther Lake processors, Reuters reported last year. Intel executives have said its yields are improving monthly and will pave the way for the launch this year.

For its part, AMD plans to give a CES keynote address at 9:30 p.m. EST on Monday  (0230 GMT on Tuesday). CEO Lisa Su will likely launch new generations of PC chips that are geared for AI and graphics.

AMD announced a multibillion-dollar deal with OpenAI for its next-generation MI400 chips, some of which the companies plan to deploy this year. The deal with the ChatGPT maker is expected to generate tens of billions of dollars in revenue for the chip designer.

The CEO of AI chip leader Nvidia, Jensen Huang, also spoke at CES on Monday. He said the company’s next generation of chips was in “full production,” and they could deliver five times the artificial-intelligence computing of the company’s previous chips when serving up chatbots and other AI apps.

title: Mercedes to offer autonomous driving tech for US city streets
content:
Jan 5 (Reuters) - Mercedes-Benz said on Monday it will launch a new advanced driver-assistance system in the United States later this year that lets its vehicles operate autonomously on city streets under driver supervision.

The system, which enables a vehicle to drive from a parking lot to a destination, navigating city intersections, making turns and obeying traffic lights, is likely to pose competition to Tesla, the only automaker that offers a similar product, called Full Self-Driving, in the United States.

Mercedes’ system, called MB.DRIVE ASSIST PRO, has been on sale in China since late last year.

The system will cost $3,950 for three years in the United States. Customer can also choose on a monthly or yearly subscriptions, but the pricing for those will be disclosed later.

Tesla’s Full Self-Driving package costs about $8,000 as a one-time purchase or $99 per month as a subscription.

Most automakers limit self-driving features in personal vehicles to highways, where traffic patterns are more predictable. Cities pose tougher challenges, including pedestrians, cyclists and unexpected situations.

Tesla is the only automaker that, with its Full Self-Driving system, allows self-driving on city streets.

But like Tesla, Mercedes’ system will require drivers to remain alert and ready to intervene at all times.

Mercedes’ push into urban driving assistance shows how software advances are moving autonomous technology from limited testing toward commercial rollout. Safety concerns and regulation still constrain full autonomy in personal vehicles.

Tesla Chief Executive Elon Musk previously said he would flood city streets with autonomous vehicles that needed no human intervention. That has not happened yet.

Instead, Tesla has focused on incremental improvements in FSD and has launched a small robotaxi service in Austin, Texas with safety monitors.

Investors still view autonomous technology as a potential long-term revenue driver for automakers.

Mercedes said the system uses about 30 sensors, including cameras, radar and ultrasonic sensors. Those sensors feed data to a computer that can process up to 508 trillion operations per second.

Nvidia said the new Mercedes-Benz CLA, the brand’s first vehicle featuring the MB.OS platform, will feature driver-assistance features powered by the chip designer’s "DRIVE AV" software, AI infrastructure and accelerated compute.

The system supports over-the-air updates for future improvements to the autonomous driving tech.

title: Lucid, Nuro, Uber debut robotaxi ahead of launch this year
content:
LAS VEGAS, Jan 5 (Reuters) - Lucid Group, Nuro and Uber on Monday unveiled a production-intent robotaxi, marking a key step toward commercial deployment for the autonomous vehicle partnership.

The unveiling at the CES technology conference in Las Vegas places the trio among a growing group of companies pursuing large-scale robotaxi deployment in the U.S., alongside Alphabet’s Waymo and Elon Musk’s Tesla among others.

The companies said on-road testing began in December, led by Nuro using engineering prototypes supervised by safety operators, a move toward a planned robotaxi launch in the San Francisco Bay Area later this year.

The testing program combines real-world driving, closed-course testing and simulation to validate safety before commercial deployment. Production of the robotaxi is expected to begin later this year at Lucid’s Arizona factory, pending final validation.

Uber, which sold its self-driving unit to Aurora Innovation in 2020, has since repositioned itself as a platform partner for a slew of developers of self-driving technology. It has signed multiple deals to bring robotaxis onto its app rather than owning the technology outright.

For Lucid, the project represents a bid to diversify beyond consumer electric vehicles, as the luxury EV maker grapples with slowing demand in the U.S. EV market, heavy cash burn and intensifying competition. The company earlier in the day reported 2025 deliveries slightly above estimates, with production far outpacing sales.

The vehicle is based on Lucid’s Gravity electric SUV and features a roof-mounted sensor "halo" integrating cameras, lidar and radar to provide 360-degree perception. Uber designed the in-cabin experience, which includes interactive screens allowing riders to control climate, seating and entertainment, and contact support if needed.

The robotaxi uses Nuro’s Level 4 autonomous system, capable of operating without human intervention under defined conditions, and runs on Nvidia’s DRIVE AGX Thor computing platform.

title: Aeva’s 4D LiDAR selected for NVIDIA autonomous vehicle platform
content:
Aeva Technologies Inc. (AEVA) announced that its 4D LiDAR technology has been selected as the reference sensor for NVIDIA’s DRIVE Hyperion autonomous vehicle platform. The announcement was made at CES 2026 in Las Vegas.

NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion is an autonomous vehicle development platform that combines sensors including LiDAR, radar, cameras, ultrasonics and microphones with NVIDIA Drive AGX Thor and NVIDIA DriveOS operating system to enable Level 3 and Level 4 automated driving features.

Aeva’s Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) 4D LiDAR technology will serve as the LiDAR component within the Hyperion sensor suite. The technology provides 3D sensing and instant velocity measurement capabilities designed for automotive applications.

"We’re honored that Aeva’s 4D LiDAR is the LiDAR sensor for the Hyperion vehicle platform," said Soroush Salehian, Co-Founder and CEO of Aeva.

The companies plan to collaborate on integrating Aeva’s technology platform into Hyperion to support production vehicle programs targeted for 2028. This integration follows Aeva’s production agreement with a European passenger vehicle manufacturer.

Aeva’s sensors are built on a silicon-photonics LiDAR-on-Chip architecture and are designed for automotive-grade reliability and high-volume manufacturing. The technology is intended to provide detection and tracking capabilities for both day and night driving conditions.

The information was disclosed in a company press release statement.

title: Supermicro announces support for NVIDIA Vera Rubin platforms
content:
Super Micro Computer Inc. (SMCI) announced it will support NVIDIA’s upcoming Vera Rubin NVL72 and HGX Rubin NVL8 platforms, along with expanded manufacturing capacity for liquid-cooled AI solutions.

The company said it will deliver the NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 SuperCluster, a rack-scale system that combines 72 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs and 36 NVIDIA Vera CPUs. The system delivers 3.6 exaflops NVFP4 performance, 1.4 PB/s HBM4 bandwidth, and 75 TB of memory. Supermicro will also offer 2U liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX Rubin NVL8 systems featuring eight GPUs optimized for AI and high-performance computing workloads.

"Supermicro’s long-standing partnership with NVIDIA and our agile building block solutions enable us to bring the most advanced AI platforms to market faster than others," said Charles Liang, president and CEO of Supermicro. "With expanded manufacturing and industry-leading liquid-cooling expertise, we’re empowering hyperscalers and enterprises to deploy the NVIDIA Vera Rubin and Rubin platforms infrastructure at scale with unmatched speed, efficiency, and reliability."

The NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform includes NVIDIA NVLink 6 interconnects, NVIDIA Vera CPU with 88 cores and 176 threads, a third-generation Transformer Engine, and confidential computing capabilities. The platform also supports newly announced NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics networking.

Supermicro stated it has made strategic investments in expanded manufacturing facilities and liquid-cooling technology to support production and deployment of the NVIDIA platforms. The company’s Data Center Building Block Solutions approach is designed to enable customization and faster deployment times.

title: Cerence secures automaker deals for AI assistant platform
content:
Cerence Inc. (CRNC) announced that multiple global automakers have selected its xUI platform to power in-car assistants in production vehicles launching in 2026, according to a company statement.

The xUI platform combines Cerence’s artificial intelligence technology with NVIDIA AI Enterprise software and runs on Microsoft Azure cloud infrastructure. The system uses NVIDIA NIM microservices and is designed to deliver conversational AI experiences in vehicles.

Cerence and NVIDIA expanded their collaboration in January 2025 to develop CaLLM, Cerence’s automotive-focused large language models that power the xUI platform. The companies use NVIDIA AI supercomputing on Azure along with NVIDIA’s AI Enterprise software suite, including the NeMo framework.

"Automakers are moving quickly to deliver conversational AI experiences that feel natural, intelligent, and helpful to drivers," said Nils Schanz, executive vice president of product and technology at Cerence.

The platform allows automakers to deploy AI-powered features to vehicles already on the road through software updates. Cerence states this capability enables continuous introduction of new features throughout a vehicle’s lifecycle.

"Built on NVIDIA AI Enterprise and NVIDIA NIM on Microsoft Azure, Cerence delivers low-latency, production-ready AI optimized for the vehicle," said Rishi Dhall, vice president at NVIDIA.

Cerence reports that more than 525 million cars have been shipped with its technology. The Burlington, Massachusetts-based company focuses on AI-powered automotive experiences and partners with automakers and technology companies.

The company did not disclose the names of the automakers that selected the xUI platform or provide specific deployment timelines beyond the 2026 launch year.

title: CoreWeave to deploy NVIDIA Rubin platform in second half of 2026
content:
CoreWeave Inc. (CRWV) announced it will integrate NVIDIA’s Rubin technology into its AI cloud platform, with deployment expected in the second half of 2026. The company stated it will be among the first cloud providers to offer the NVIDIA Rubin platform to customers.

The NVIDIA Rubin platform is designed to support workloads including agentic AI, drug discovery, genomic research, climate simulation, and fusion energy modeling. CoreWeave plans to use the technology to expand computing performance and efficiency for enterprises, AI labs, and startups running production AI workloads.

"The NVIDIA Rubin platform represents an important advancement as AI evolves toward more sophisticated reasoning and agentic use cases," said Michael Intrator, CoreWeave’s co-founder, chairman and chief executive officer.

Jensen Huang, NVIDIA’s founder and chief executive officer, said CoreWeave will help deploy the technology as "one of the first to deploy it later this year."

CoreWeave previously launched general availability of NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 instances and the NVIDIA Grace Blackwell Ultra NVL72 platform. The company will deploy NVIDIA Rubin using its CoreWeave Mission Control operating standard, which integrates security, operations and observability features.

The deployment will utilize CoreWeave’s Rack Lifecycle Controller, a system that manages NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 racks as single programmable units. This system coordinates provisioning, power operations and hardware validation.

CoreWeave completed its public listing on Nasdaq in March 2025, according to the press release. The company was established in 2017 and provides AI cloud computing services.

title: Nvidia unveils BlueField-4 powered AI storage platform for agentic systems
content:
LAS VEGAS - Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) announced today its new Inference Context Memory Storage Platform, powered by the BlueField-4 data processor, designed to address storage challenges in advanced AI systems. The semiconductor giant, with a market capitalization of $4.56 trillion, continues to expand its AI infrastructure offerings amid impressive 65.22% revenue growth over the last twelve months.

"AI is revolutionizing the entire computing stack — and now, storage," said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of Nvidia, in a press release statement. "AI is no longer about one-shot chatbots but intelligent collaborators that understand the physical world, reason over long horizons, stay grounded in facts, use tools to do real work, and retain both short- and long-term memory."

The platform utilizes Nvidia’s Rubin cluster-level KV cache capacity for long-context processing and multi-turn agentic inference. It integrates with Nvidia’s DOCA framework and NIXL library to maximize performance and improve responsiveness in multi-agent systems.

Nvidia Spectrum-X Ethernet serves as the network fabric for the platform, enabling efficient data sharing and retrieval through RDMA-based access to AI-native KV cache.

Several storage companies including Dell Technologies, HPE, IBM, Pure Storage, and WEKA are among the first partners building next-generation AI storage platforms with BlueField-4, which Nvidia said will be available in the second half of 2026.

The announcement comes as AI models continue to scale to trillions of parameters, generating increasing amounts of context data that cannot be efficiently stored long-term on GPUs alone.

In other recent news, Nvidia introduced its Rubin platform, which features six new chips designed to significantly reduce costs and enhance performance for AI computing. The Rubin platform is reported to cut inference token costs by up to ten times and requires four times fewer GPUs for training mixture-of-experts models compared to its predecessor, the Blackwell platform. Additionally, Nvidia unveiled new AI models and robotics technology at CES 2026, including open models available on Hugging Face and frameworks for physical AI, aimed at improving robots’ interaction with the physical world. The company also launched the Alpamayo family of open AI models, designed to enhance autonomous vehicle safety by addressing complex driving scenarios. Arbe Robotics announced a new radar platform integrating Nvidia’s computing capabilities, showcasing its automotive-grade radar system at CES 2026, which is ready for integration with advanced perception and AI algorithms. Meanwhile, CLSA raised its price target for Elite Material Co Ltd., highlighting its potential benefits from the adoption of Nvidia’s Rubin generation technology in various components. These developments reflect Nvidia’s ongoing efforts to advance AI technology across multiple sectors.

title: NVIDIA launches BlueField-4 for AI-native storage infrastructure
content:
NVIDIA (NVDA) announced the NVIDIA BlueField-4 data processor powers its new Inference Context Memory Storage Platform, designed for AI applications requiring extended memory capabilities.

The platform addresses storage challenges as AI models scale to trillions of parameters and generate large amounts of context data through key-value caches. These caches cannot be stored on GPUs long-term without creating bottlenecks for real-time inference in multi-agent systems.

The company states the platform extends GPU memory capacity and enables high-speed data sharing across nodes. NVIDIA claims the system delivers up to 5x greater power efficiency and boosts tokens per second by up to 5x compared to traditional storage systems.

"AI is revolutionizing the entire computing stack — and now, storage," said Jensen Huang, NVIDIA’s founder and CEO. "AI is no longer about one-shot chatbots but intelligent collaborators that understand the physical world, reason over long horizons, stay grounded in facts, use tools to do real work, and retain both short- and long-term memory."

The platform features cluster-level KV cache capacity for the NVIDIA Rubin platform, hardware-accelerated cache placement managed by BlueField-4, and data sharing enabled by NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet networking.

Storage companies including AIC, Cloudian, DDN, Dell Technologies, HPE, Hitachi Vantara, IBM, Nutanix, Pure Storage, Supermicro, VAST Data and WEKA are developing next-generation AI storage platforms using BlueField-4.

The BlueField-4 platform will be available in the second half of 2026, according to the company’s announcement at CES.

title: Nvidia unveils Rubin platform with 10x lower inference token costs
content:
LAS VEGAS - Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) introduced its next-generation AI platform called Rubin on Monday, featuring six new chips designed to deliver significant cost and performance improvements for AI computing. The semiconductor giant, with a market capitalization of $4.56 trillion and impressive revenue growth of 65.22% in the last twelve months, continues to strengthen its position as a dominant force in AI hardware.

The Rubin platform claims to reduce inference token costs by up to 10 times and requires 4 times fewer GPUs to train mixture-of-experts (MoE) models compared to Nvidia’s previous Blackwell platform, according to a company press release.

The system includes the Nvidia Vera CPU, Rubin GPU, NVLink 6 Switch, ConnectX-9 SuperNIC, BlueField-4 DPU, and Spectrum-6 Ethernet Switch - all designed through what Nvidia calls "extreme codesign" to improve AI training and inference efficiency.

"Rubin arrives at exactly the right moment, as AI computing demand for both training and inference is going through the roof," said Jensen Huang, Nvidia’s founder and CEO.

The platform introduces five key innovations including the latest generations of NVLink interconnect technology, Transformer Engine, Confidential Computing, RAS Engine, and the Vera CPU. These technologies aim to accelerate agentic AI, reasoning capabilities, and large-scale model inference.

Major technology companies including Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure plan to deploy Rubin-based systems. AI companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta have also expressed interest in utilizing the platform.

Microsoft will deploy Nvidia Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems in its next-generation AI data centers, including future Fairwater AI superfactory sites.

The Rubin platform is in production with partner products expected to be available in the second half of 2026. CoreWeave will be among the first cloud providers to integrate Rubin-based systems into its AI cloud platform.

Nvidia also announced the Inference Context Memory Storage Platform with BlueField-4 storage processor to accelerate agentic AI reasoning, and Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics switch systems that deliver 5x improved power efficiency.

In other recent news, NVIDIA announced the release of new open AI models and robotics technology at CES 2026, including models like NVIDIA Cosmos Transfer 2.5 and Cosmos Predict 2.5, which aim to enhance robots’ interaction with the physical world. The company also introduced the Alpamayo family of open AI models, designed to address safety challenges in autonomous vehicles by improving reasoning in complex driving scenarios. Additionally, NVIDIA entered into a $20 billion non-exclusive licensing agreement with AI startup Groq, granting access to Groq’s Language Processing Units for inference tasks. Mizuho reiterated its Outperform rating for NVIDIA, maintaining a $245.00 price target. Arbe Robotics unveiled a new radar platform powered by NVIDIA technology, which will be showcased at CES 2026. This platform delivers advanced perception capabilities through a 2,304-channel array. Meanwhile, CLSA raised its price target for Elite Material Co Ltd., citing potential benefits from NVIDIA’s Rubin generation technology. These developments highlight NVIDIA’s ongoing efforts to innovate and expand its influence in AI and autonomous technology sectors.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Nvidia plans to test own robotaxis by 2027
content:
Investing.com -- Nvidia unveiled plans on Monday to test a robotaxi service with a partner as soon as 2027, marking a significant step in the chipmaker’s push to become a key player in the self-driving car market.

The planned service would utilize vehicles equipped with "Level 4" driving capabilities, allowing them to operate without human intervention within pre-defined geographic areas. Nvidia has not yet disclosed the location of the service or identified its partner for this initiative.

"We will probably start with a limited availability but work with the partner for us to get our footing," said Xinzhou Wu, Nvidia’s vice president of automotive, during a self-driving demonstration held in San Francisco last month.

At the CES conference in Las Vegas on Monday, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang shared his vision for autonomous vehicles: "We imagine that someday, a billion cars on the road will all be autonomous. You could either have it be a robotaxi that you’re orchestrating and renting from somebody, or you could own it."

Huang has previously identified robotics, including self-driving cars, as the company’s second most important growth category after artificial intelligence.

title: NVIDIA unveils new AI models and robotics hardware at CES
content:
NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) announced new open-source artificial intelligence models and robotics hardware at the Consumer Electronics Show, alongside robot debuts from global partners including Boston Dynamics, Caterpillar and LG Electronics.

The company released several new models on Hugging Face, including NVIDIA Cosmos Transfer 2.5 and Cosmos Predict 2.5 world models for synthetic data generation and robot policy evaluation. NVIDIA also introduced Cosmos Reason 2, a vision language model, and Isaac GR00T N1.6, designed specifically for humanoid robot control.

The new NVIDIA Jetson T4000 module, powered by Blackwell architecture, is now available for $1,999 at 1,000-unit volume. The module delivers four times greater energy efficiency than the previous generation with 1,200 FP4 TFLOPS and 64GB of memory within a 70-watt power envelope.

NVIDIA released two new open-source frameworks on GitHub: Isaac Lab-Arena for robot policy evaluation and benchmarking, and OSMO, a cloud-native orchestration framework for robotic development workflows. The company partnered with Hugging Face to integrate these technologies into the LeRobot open-source robotics framework.

Several companies demonstrated new robots using NVIDIA technologies at CES. NEURA Robotics launched a Porsche-designed humanoid robot, while Richtech Robotics introduced Dex, a mobile humanoid for industrial environments. LG Electronics unveiled a home robot for household tasks, and Boston Dynamics, Humanoid and RLWRLD integrated Jetson Thor into their existing humanoid platforms.

Caterpillar is expanding its collaboration with NVIDIA to bring AI and autonomy to construction and mining equipment. The partnership details will be discussed during a CES keynote on January 7.

NVIDIA IGX Thor will become available later this month for industrial edge computing applications. Multiple hardware partners including AAEON, Advantech, ADLINK and others offer Thor-powered systems for edge AI and robotics applications.

title: NVIDIA brings DRIVE AV software to US roads with Mercedes-Benz CLA
content:
NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) announced plans to deploy its DRIVE AV software with enhanced level 2 driver assistance capabilities on U.S. roads by the end of this year, starting with Mercedes-Benz vehicles.

The Mercedes-Benz CLA will be the first vehicle to feature NVIDIA’s full-stack DRIVE AV software as part of the automaker’s new MB.OS platform. The CLA recently received a five-star European New Car Assessment Program EuroNCAP safety rating.

The DRIVE AV system uses what NVIDIA describes as a dual-stack architecture combining an AI end-to-end stack for core driving functions with a parallel classical safety stack built on the company’s Halos safety system. The technology enables point-to-point urban navigation, automated parking, and cooperative steering between the system and driver.

"As the automotive industry embraces physical AI, NVIDIA is the intelligence backbone that makes every vehicle programmable, updatable and perpetually improving through data and software," said Ali Kani, vice president of automotive at NVIDIA.

The system’s deep learning models are designed to interpret traffic conditions and navigate through lane selection, turns, and route-following in urban environments. The technology also aims to identify pedestrians, cyclists, and scooter riders to help prevent collisions.

NVIDIA and Mercedes-Benz are also implementing digital manufacturing approaches using NVIDIA Omniverse libraries to create digital twins of factories and assembly lines for virtual optimization.

The development process involves three components: NVIDIA DGX systems for training AI models, Omniverse and Cosmos simulation environments for testing, and DRIVE AGX compute systems for in-vehicle processing. NVIDIA states it collaborates with multiple global automakers to integrate its driving technologies across various vehicle platforms.

title: NVIDIA releases open AI models spanning robotics to healthcare
content:
NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) released a collection of open AI models, datasets and development tools across multiple industries including autonomous vehicles, robotics, healthcare and enterprise applications, according to a company statement.

The release includes models from the Nemotron family for enterprise AI applications, the Cosmos platform for physical AI systems, the new Alpamayo family for autonomous vehicle development, Isaac GR00T for robotics and Clara models for biomedical applications.

The company made available training frameworks and datasets including 10 trillion language training tokens, 500,000 robotics trajectories, 455,000 protein structures and 100 terabytes of vehicle sensor data.

Technology companies including Bosch, CrowdStrike, Cohesity, Fortinet, Franka Robotics, Palantir, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Hitachi and Uber are implementing these open model technologies in their products and services.

The Nemotron family includes speech recognition models, multimodal retrieval systems and safety models. Bosch is using Nemotron Speech for vehicle interaction systems, while ServiceNow incorporates the technology in its Apriel model family. CrowdStrike, Cohesity and Fortinet are adopting Nemotron Safety models for their AI applications.

The Cosmos platform offers world foundation models for physical AI development. Cosmos Reason 2 provides vision-language capabilities for robots and AI agents, while Cosmos Transfer 2.5 and Cosmos Predict 2.5 generate synthetic videos across different environments.

The Alpamayo family targets autonomous vehicle development with reasoning-based models and simulation tools. It includes Alpamayo 1, described as the first open large-scale reasoning model for autonomous vehicles, and AlpaSim, a simulation framework for training and evaluation.

Clara AI models for healthcare include La-Proteina for protein design, ReaSyn v2 for drug synthesis planning, KERMT for computational safety testing and RNAPro for RNA molecule structure prediction.

title: nvidia unveils open ai models to advance autonomous vehicle safety
content:
LAS VEGAS - NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) introduced its Alpamayo family of open AI models, simulation tools and datasets aimed at addressing autonomous vehicle safety challenges, according to a company press release issued Monday. The semiconductor giant, with a market capitalization of $4.56 trillion and revenue of $187.14 billion over the last twelve months, continues to expand its AI portfolio beyond its core chip business.

The new offering includes Alpamayo 1, a 10-billion-parameter vision language action model designed to help autonomous vehicles reason through complex driving scenarios, particularly rare "long-tail" situations that have proven difficult for traditional autonomous systems.

The Alpamayo package also features AlpaSim, an open-source simulation framework for testing autonomous driving systems, and Physical AI Open Datasets containing over 1,700 hours of driving data from diverse geographical locations and conditions.

"The ChatGPT moment for physical AI is here," said Jensen Huang, NVIDIA’s founder and CEO. "Alpamayo brings reasoning to autonomous vehicles, allowing them to think through rare scenarios, drive safely in complex environments and explain their driving decisions."

Unlike traditional autonomous vehicle systems that separate perception and planning functions, Alpamayo uses chain-of-thought reasoning to generate driving decisions with explanations of the logic behind each action, which the company says improves safety and transparency.

Several automotive and mobility companies have expressed interest in the technology, including Lucid Motors, JLR, and Uber, along with research institutions like Berkeley DeepDrive.

The Alpamayo 1 model is now available on Hugging Face, while the AlpaSim framework can be accessed through GitHub. NVIDIA stated that future releases will feature larger parameter counts and enhanced reasoning capabilities.

The company positions these tools as foundational elements that developers can fine-tune and integrate into their autonomous vehicle development stacks rather than as direct in-vehicle systems.

In other recent news, Nvidia has announced a significant $20 billion non-exclusive licensing deal with AI startup Groq, granting Nvidia access to Groq’s Language Processing Units for inference tasks. Mizuho has reiterated its Outperform rating for Nvidia, maintaining a price target of $245.00, while Bernstein SocGen Group also reaffirmed an Outperform rating, setting a higher price target of $275.00. This deal highlights Nvidia’s strategic focus on inference, which is expected to represent a substantial portion of future workloads. Meanwhile, Arbe Robotics has unveiled a new radar platform powered by NVIDIA’s computing capabilities, which will be showcased at CES 2026. This platform is designed to integrate with complete perception stacks, delivering over 20,000 detections per frame. In related developments, CLSA has raised its price target for Elite Material Co Ltd. to NT$2,130.00, citing the company’s strong market leadership and potential benefits from the adoption of M9-grade CCL in Nvidia’s Rubin generation. These recent developments underscore ongoing strategic moves and technological advancements within the industry.

title: NVIDIA releases open-source AI models for autonomous vehicle development
content:
NVIDIA (NVDA) announced the Alpamayo family of open-source artificial intelligence models and tools designed for autonomous vehicle development at CES in Las Vegas.

The Alpamayo family includes three components: Alpamayo 1, a 10-billion-parameter vision language action model; AlpaSim, an open-source simulation framework; and Physical AI Open Datasets containing 1,700+ hours of driving data across various geographic locations and conditions.

Alpamayo 1 represents what the company describes as a chain-of-thought reasoning model that generates driving trajectories alongside reasoning traces. The model is available on Hugging Face with open model weights and open-source inferencing scripts. AlpaSim is available on GitHub as a simulation framework for autonomous vehicle development and testing.

The Physical AI Open Datasets, also available on Hugging Face, contain driving data collected across multiple geographies and driving conditions, including scenarios the company characterizes as edge cases for autonomous vehicle systems.

Several automotive industry companies have expressed support for the initiative. Lucid Motors, JLR, and Uber provided statements regarding the potential applications of the technology for autonomous vehicle development.

"The shift toward physical AI highlights the growing need for AI systems that can reason about real-world behavior, not just process data," said Kai Stepper, vice president of ADAS and autonomous driving at Lucid Motors.

The models are designed to serve as teacher models that developers can adapt and integrate into their autonomous vehicle development processes. NVIDIA indicated that future models in the Alpamayo family will feature larger parameter counts and additional capabilities.

The announcement was made during NVIDIA’s presentation at CES, with the tools becoming available through the specified open-source platforms.

title: TSX jumps with U.S. Venezuela strike in focus
content:
Investing.com - Canada’s main stock exchange jumped on Monday, a positive start to the first full trading week of 2026 as investors assessed the implications of an audacious U.S. strike against Venezuela.

The S&P/TSX composite index jumped 336 points or 1.06% at 32,219.95.

Index gained 170.61 points, or 0.5%, at 31,883.37 on Friday, kicking off the new year on an upbeat note. In 2025, the average climbed by 28.25%, notching its largest increase in 16 years.

Despite headwinds facing the Canadian economy, an increase in gold prices, as well as a decline in domestic interest rates and ongoing enthusiasm around artificial intelligence, helped to mitigate these pressures last year.

U.S. stocks higher with Venezuela strikes in focus

The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average had risen by 466 points, or 1.0%, the benchmark S&P 500 had increased by 38 points, or 0.6%, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite had ticked up by 132 points, or 0.5%.

The benchmark S&P 500 and blue chip Dow Jones Industrial Average both eked out gains on Friday, as the inaugural session of 2026 was highlighted by advances in shares of semiconductor giants Nvidia and Intel. Both indices snapped four-day losing streaks in the process.

Analysts are eager to see if U.S. stocks can post yet another year of increases, after all three of the major averages on Wall Street notched double-digit jumps in 2025. It was the third consecutive year in the green for the S&P, Dow, and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, matching a run previously recorded between 2019-2021.

Crude prices choppy

Crude prices whipsawed in European trade on Monday after the U.S. over the weekend captured Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro and said it was taking temporary control of the Latin American country.

Brent oil futures rose 0.1% to $60.81 a barrel by 06:04 ET, while West Texas Intermediate crude futures added 0.2% to $57.44 a barrel. Prices had slumped earlier in the day.

Oil prices were nursing an over 18% slide in 2025, their worst in five years, as fears of a supply glut and weakening demand battered crude markets.

U.S. forces captured Maduro in a weekend strike, with the Venezuelan leader now set to face drug-trafficking charges in New York. President Donald Trump said that Washington will run Venezuela until a new leader is elected, and that as part of the incursion, major U.S. oil companies will be allowed to move into the country.

Shares of U.S. oil groups Chevron, ExxonMobil, and ConocoPhillips all surged in premarket trading.

Venezuela has the largest proven oil reserves in the world. But output from the country has faltered due to aging infrastructure and strict U.S. sanctions.

Much debate is now swirling around the implications of a potential U.S.-driven regime change in Venezuela, a source of uncertainty that has also burnished the appeal of "the liquidity of the dollar," analysts at ING.

Trump on Sunday floated the possibility of a second military strike on Venezuela, if the country’s interim administration did not cooperate.

Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump also flagged potential action against Colombia over its role in the drug trade, and said Cuba was ready to fall. He reiterated as well his view that the U.S. must control Greenland for national security reasons.

Gold prices gain

Gold prices spiked in European trading following a fresh bout of safe-haven buying prompted by the U.S. military operation in Venezuela.

Spot gold was last up 1.9% at $4,414.40 an ounce by 07:21 ET. U.S. Gold Futures for March delivery climbed 2.2% to $4,426.10.

Gold prices surged over 60% in 2025, reaching a record high of $4,549.71/oz, but heavy profit-taking after that weighed on bullion. Prices have rebounded since then, and are not far from peak levels.

title: Hyundai Motor Group plans to deploy humanoid robots at US factory from 2028
content:
Jan 6 (Reuters) - Hyundai Motor Group plans to deploy humanoid robots at its U.S. manufacturing plant in Georgia starting in 2028, marking a step toward automating higher-risk and repetitive manufacturing tasks, the South Korean company said.

Hyundai unveiled the production version of the Atlas humanoid robot, developed by its unit Boston Dynamics, at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas.

The company did not disclose the volume or cost of the robots, but said in a statement it aims to roll out adoption across all of its manufacturing sites as part of a push into "physical AI".

The robots will initially carry out parts sequencing tasks from 2028, with applications expanding gradually as safety and quality benefits are validated, it said.

By 2030, Hyundai said Atlas robots were expected to move into component assembly with a longer-term plan to take on tasks involving heavy loads, repetitive motions and complex operations across production sites.

The robots are designed to reduce physical strain on workers by taking on higher-risk and repetitive tasks, laying the groundwork for broader commercial use in industrial settings, it said.

At Hyundai Motor’s affiliate Kia Corp, the labour union last year called to establish a body to address potential labour rights issues in preparation for the AI era, as workers raised concerns about expanding automation.

The company expects humanoid robots to become the largest segment of the physical artificial intelligence market, which refers to AI systems embedded in hardware that collect real-world data and make autonomous decisions, spanning areas such as robotics, smart factories and autonomous driving.

Atlas features human-scale hands with tactile sensing and has the ability to lift up to 50 kg (110 pounds), according to Hyundai.

The robot can operate autonomously and is designed to function in industrial environments ranging from minus 20 degrees Celsius to 40 degrees Celsius.

Hyundai said it is accelerating development in this area through partnerships with global AI leaders, including a collaboration with Nvidia and Google, aimed at improving safety, efficiency and real-world deployment.

title: BofA says these are the best 7 AI chip stocks to own in 2026
content:
Investing.com -- Bank of America remains “constructive on AI-related semi, memory and semicap stocks despite expected volatility,” highlighting seven names it sees as best positioned for 2026.

In a note Monday, BofA analyst Vivek Arya said this year’s CES may reinforce new drivers in robotics and on-device AI alongside ongoing cloud expansion.

BofA flagged Nvidia, Broadcom, Lam Research, KLA, AMD, Credo Technology and Teradyne as the firms best placed to benefit, citing its 2026 outlook report.

The AI buildout remains “mid-cycle,” BofA said, noting that prior platform cycles “lasted 5–10 years.”

It added that data-center capital expenditure is “more durable—mission-critical and well-funded—than investment alternatives in cyclical auto, industrial, or consumer markets.”

Valuations also remain attractive, with BofA stating that AI leaders still trade around “~1x PEG vs. 1.5x–2.0x for SPX/large-cap peers.”

While bubble concerns persist, the bank argued that “risks are already well understood and priced.”

Nvidia was singled out as “best-positioned,” with BofA citing its strategy to optimise the entire AI stack from networking to GPU workload management.

The firm’s supply-chain alignment could give it an edge “in a wafer- and memory-constrained environment.”

BofA believes investors should watch utilisation levels, cloud free-cash-flow cushions and AI adoption trends, which “matter more than noise around bubbles, LLM commoditization, lower training costs, [and] China competition.”

The bank also sees robotics, on-device AI and emerging “World Models” as expanding themes heading into 2026.

title: Dow Jones, Nasdaq, S&P 500 weekly preview: Markets face busy start to 2026
content:
Investing.com -- U.S. stocks ended the first session of 2026 modestly higher, supported by strength in semiconductor shares that helped offset broader weakness across technology.

The S&P 500 edged up 0.19% to 6,858.47, while the Nasdaq Composite slipped 0.03% to 23,235.63. Both indexes were well off their intraday highs after opening the session strongly, with earlier gains of about 0.7% for the S&P 500 and roughly 1.5% for the Nasdaq. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 319 points, or 0.66%, to 48,382.39.

Chipmakers were the main source of support. Nvidia added more than 1%, while Micron Technology surged over 10%, continuing momentum from last year’s rally in artificial intelligence-linked stocks. Both names were standout performers in 2025, with Nvidia climbing around 39% and Micron more than tripling.

Outside of semiconductors, other parts of the technology sector struggled, with software stocks weighing on the Nasdaq’s performance and limiting broader gains.

Technology was the top-performing sector in 2025, driving strong returns across major benchmarks as investors continued to favor AI-exposed names.

The S&P 500 rose more than 16% last year, extending its winning streak to three consecutive years. The Nasdaq advanced over 20%, while the Dow gained roughly 13%. All three hit record highs in 2025.

What could move markets in the first full week of 2026

The first full trading week of the new year may bring U.S. markets out of the holiday lull as investors confront a packed macro and geopolitical calendar, with recent stunning developments in Venezuela and key labor data in focus.

Attention turned to Venezuela over the weekend after U.S. President Donald Trump said he was placing the country under temporary American control following the capture of President Nicolas Maduro.

Investors said the situation adds a new layer of geopolitical risk, particularly for energy markets, with any sharp swings in oil prices likely to spill over into broader asset classes.

Geopolitics remains only one part of the picture. Markets are also bracing for a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on Trump’s tariff policies, alongside the president’s pending decision on the next Federal Reserve chair, as earnings season approaches later in the month.

The January 9 U.S. employment report is expected to be a key catalyst. Signs of labor market softness late last year pushed the Fed to cut interest rates at each of its final three meetings in 2025, as policymakers balanced slowing employment against inflation that remains above the 2% target.

"Following two volatile months of labor data, the December employment report should give Fed officials a somewhat better read on the underlying trends," Brett Ryan, senior U.S. economist at Deutsche Bank, said in a note.

Deutsche Bank expects modest job growth in the upcoming report, forecasting an increase of 50,000 in headline payrolls compared with a prior reading of 64,000, and a similar 50,000 rise in private payrolls versus 69,000 previously. The bank also projects the unemployment rate to edge down by one-tenth of a percentage point to 4.5%.

While lower rates have underpinned equities, the outlook for further easing in 2026 is uncertain. Fed officials showed clear divisions in December, and with policy rates at 3.5% to 3.75%, futures markets point to limited odds of a January cut and roughly even chances of a quarter-point move in March.

At the same time, investors are cautious that a sharply weaker jobs report could raise broader growth concerns. Additional releases this week, including manufacturing and services activity and job openings data, are expected to offer a fuller picture as economic reporting returns to normal following a prolonged government shutdown.

What analysts are saying about U.S. stocks

Evercore ISI: "In our 2026 outlook, we made the case that the Structural Bull Market has further to run in the year ahead, powered by even more of what investors experienced in 2025. More AI as inflection in cost savings and revenue generation turns to adoption Acceleration. More Stimulus as politicians prioritize Affordability in a “K-shaped” economy ahead of Midterm Elections, all while the Fed is on track to continue cutting rates. More Earnings Growth, albeit with a high bar set into the next reporting season. And more Volatility, risks appearing underpriced against historically expensive multiples – the absence of both bearish sentiment and a Santa Claus rally underscores VIX in the teens attractive for a move both through SPX 7,000 or a retest of 6,500, or both."

Morgan Stanley: "We think consensus is still underestimating the collective impact of a series of bullish catalysts from de-regulation to operating leverage to accommodative monetary & fiscal policy. The surprise for ’26 could be that we see multiple expansion for the median stock in addition to strong earnings."

RBC Capital Markets: "We are reiterating our 7,750 12-month S&P 500 price target. We continue to anticipate another solid year of S&P 500 performance (roughly 13% vs. the 12/31/2025 close), supported by continued solid economic growth, additional/modest easing from the Fed, and solid earnings growth."

title: Cantor Fitzgerald sees semiconductor sector outperforming in 2026
content:
Investing.com - Cantor Fitzgerald expects the semiconductor sector to outperform the broader market in 2026, driven by artificial intelligence (AI) computing demand and supply constraints across memory and storage components. This outlook aligns with the sector’s recent momentum, exemplified by companies like Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) which has delivered a remarkable 148.64% return over the past year.

The investment firm predicts that DRAM, NAND, and hard disk drives will face sustained supply constraints, supporting what it describes as a "capacity-driven upcycle" that could boost semiconductor capital equipment company estimates.

Cantor Fitzgerald identified several top picks across different semiconductor segments, including NASDAQ:NVDA and NASDAQ:AVGO in AI computing, NASDAQ:MU in DRAM memory, and NASDAQ:WDC and NASDAQ:STX in storage.

In the semiconductor equipment space, the firm favors NASDAQ:ASML, NYSE:AMAT, NASDAQ:LRCX, NASDAQ:KLAC, and NASDAQ:MKSI, while also highlighting NASDAQ:TER and NASDAQ:FORM in back-end testing and equipment.

In other recent news, Lam Research Corporation announced its Board of Directors approved a quarterly dividend of $0.26 per share, payable on January 7, 2026, to shareholders on record by December 3, 2025. TD Cowen raised its price target for Lam Research to $170 from $145, maintaining a Buy rating due to strong performance in the September quarter, driven by demand from China. Cantor Fitzgerald reiterated its Overweight rating and $170 price target, noting that despite new export restrictions, Lam is expected to achieve earnings per share of $4.80 for 2025, surpassing the consensus estimate. Bernstein SocGen Group also increased its price target for Lam Research to $170 from $105, citing optimism about growth in wafer fabrication equipment. Additionally, Lam Research opened a new $65 million office building in Tualatin, Oregon, as part of its strategy to expand facilities in anticipation of semiconductor industry growth.

title: Arbe Robotics stock soars after unveiling NVIDIA-powered radar platform
content:
Investing.com -- Arbe Robotics Ltd. (NASDAQ:ARBE) stock soared 20.8% in Monday premarket trading after the company announced a new radar platform that combines its technology with NVIDIA’s computing capabilities.

The Israel-based perception radar solutions provider said it will showcase its automotive-grade radar system at CES 2026, demonstrating its readiness for integration with complete perception stacks. The system delivers over 20,000 detections per frame through a 2,304-channel array, providing rich data for advanced perception and AI algorithms.

Arbe’s radar technology aims to enable hands-off and eyes-off driving at highway speeds by detecting objects at distances over 300 meters, giving autonomous systems sufficient time to react safely. The company emphasized that its solution maintains reliable performance across challenging environmental conditions including snow, rain, and fog.

"This initiative allows us to create a new benchmark for perception in all conditions. The collaboration accelerates the adoption of safer, more reliable, and more affordable autonomous driving solutions for automakers worldwide," said Kobi Marenko, co-founder and CEO of Arbe.

At CES 2026, Arbe plans to demonstrate several applications of its technology, including an AI-based Occupancy Grid developed with Perciv AI, LiDAR-comparable performance from its HD radar, and integration with NVIDIA’s DRIVE AGX Orin computing platform.

The company’s stock has been volatile over the past year as investors assess the potential of its radar technology in the competitive autonomous driving sensor market.

According to Needham, TSMC appears willing to outsource "excess" CoWoS demand to ASE and Amkor Technology, positioning both companies as "the biggest CoWoS leaders over the next few years." title: Arbe partners with NVIDIA for AI-based autonomous driving platform
content:
Arbe Robotics Ltd. (ARBE) announced it is combining its radar technology with NVIDIA accelerated computing to create a platform for AI-based perception in autonomous driving applications. The company will demonstrate its automotive-grade radar system at CES 2026 for the first time.

The radar system generates over 20,000 detections per frame through a 2,304-channel array, providing data for perception and AI algorithms. The technology achieves detection ranges exceeding 300 meters, which the company states is necessary for highway driving at speeds up to 130 km/h.

"This initiative allows us to create a new benchmark for perception in all conditions. The collaboration accelerates the adoption of safer, more reliable, and more affordable autonomous driving solutions for automakers worldwide," said Kobi Marenko, co-founder and CEO of Arbe.

At CES 2026, Arbe will showcase three demonstrations at booth 4551 in West Hall. These include an AI-based occupancy grid developed with Perciv AI, a comparison of its radar performance to LiDAR technology, and integration with NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin computing platform.

The radar system operates in various weather conditions including snow, rain, fog, and low-visibility scenarios. Arbe positions this as addressing limitations of other sensor types in challenging environmental conditions.

Arbe, headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, develops radar solutions for automotive applications. The company operates additional offices in the United States, Germany, and China. The announcement is based on a company press release.

title: Beamr outlines 2026 strategy focused on autonomous vehicle market
content:
Beamr Imaging Ltd. (BMR) issued a shareholder letter outlining its 2025 achievements and 2026 strategic direction, with plans to convert validated use cases into commercial agreements across autonomous vehicle, artificial intelligence and media markets.

The video optimization technology company reported entering the autonomous vehicle market in 2025, transitioning from validation to commercialization planning. CEO Sharon Carmel stated the company completed proof-of-concept evaluations with automotive suppliers and AV developers, validating up to 50% reduction in video storage while maintaining model stability with accuracy changes below 2%.

Key 2025 milestones included launching a GPU-accelerated video compression solution for autonomous vehicles at NVIDIA GTC Paris in June. The company received the NAB Show Product of the Year award in April for its AV1 codec solution and won Best of Show at IBC 2025 for a live 4K sports broadcasting demonstration.

Beamr joined Amazon Web Services’ ISV Accelerate program in February 2025 and completed SOC 2 Type II compliance certification in December. The company presented at six industry shows across media, entertainment and autonomous vehicle sectors.

For 2026, Beamr plans to focus on execution through partner-led deployments and production-ready solutions. The company aims to establish relationships with what it terms "lighthouse" accounts - global industry leaders requiring extended evaluation cycles due to video’s role in their infrastructure.

Beamr’s technology reduces video file sizes while preserving quality, targeting applications in media, user-generated content, machine learning and autonomous vehicles. The company holds 53 patents and previously won an Emmy Award for Technology and Engineering.

title: Self-driving tech, AI take center stage at CES as automakers dial back EV plans
content:
LAS VEGAS, Jan 5 (Reuters) - Autonomous driving technology is expected to dominate the CES trade show in Las Vegas this week as investors bet that artificial intelligence will invigorate an industry beset by slow progress, high costs, safety incidents and regulatory scrutiny.

Just as automakers have hit the brakes on electric vehicle (EV) plans and look for their next money maker, a slew of auto suppliers and start ups are lining up to show off their latest autonomous vehicle hardware and software. Partnerships and deals that promise to take away much of a driver’s responsibilities, or remove the need for a human driver completely, are expected to be announced.

"This year you will see more and more focus on AI and autonomous," said C.J. Finn, U.S. automotive industry leader for PwC, adding that how companies use AI to solve the challenge of rolling out driverless cars safely will be closely watched. "That connectivity on autonomous, I do think will be front and center."

AI, though, is expected to be imbued into products well beyond autos - from robots and wearable gadgets to home devices and health technology.

Tech heavyweights including AI chip giant Nvidia’s CEO Jensen Huang and AMD CEO Lisa Su are among the key speakers this year.

NO ELECTRIC VEHICLES

CES 2026, one of the largest technology exhibits in the United States, will run from Jan. 6 to 9 this year. Formerly called the Consumer Electronics Show and known traditionally as the launchpad for the latest in tech such as TVs, laptops and wearables, CES, in recent years, has emerged as a key destination for automakers debuting their EVs.

But a pullback on EV-friendly incentives and policies by the Trump administration have dampened demand and forced many automakers to abandon plans to launch new EVs and rethink their strategy.

The upheaval will be evident at CES. Most major automakers have no plans to launch any new EVs this year - a stark difference from the past few years.

MONEY ON AUTONOMY

Commercializing autonomous vehicles has been not been easy. High investments, regulatory challenges and investigations after collisions have forced many companies to shut down.

But Tesla’s launch of a small robotaxi service with safety monitors in Austin, Texas, last year as well as quicker expansion by Alphabet’s Waymo has breathed new life into the industry.

Driver-assist systems for personal vehicles have also improved, with some automakers offering hands-free driving and automatic lane change on highways. Some, such as Rivian, aim to launch "eyes-off" functionality and self-driving on city streets.

"That’s starting to align with where people are putting forward their money and how they’re allocating capital," Finn said.

COST WORRIES LINGER

Companies, especially automakers, are being strategic about capital investments after swallowing billions of dollars in write-offs due to changes in EV strategies. They are also dealing with the effects of high tariffs on auto and auto part imports imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.

Many automakers have chosen to absorb most of the cost of tariffs, instead of passing them on to customers, adding pressure on profit margins. That, along with growing competition from Chinese players, will also be top of mind for automakers at CES, said Felix Stellmaszek, global leader of the automotive and mobility sector at Boston Consulting Group.

"The main theme that we actually expect to see also popping up at CES is around cost and cost competitiveness," Stellmaszek said.

title: Five things to watch in markets in the week ahead
content:
Investing.com - A daring U.S. capture of Venezuela’s leader is in the spotlight at the outset of the first full trading week of 2026. Analysts are attempting to wrap their heads around the implications of the incursion, which will see the U.S. assuming temporary control over the oil-rich Latin American nation. Investors are also preparing for the release of key employment figures, as the schedule of economic data looks set to return to normal following a prolonged U.S. government shutdown. Elsewhere, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is expected to speak at an event on the outlook for artificial intelligence this year, while the board of Warner Bros Discovery is reportedly seen rejecting a sweetened takeover offer from Paramount Skydance.

1. Oil dips following U.S. capture of Venezuela’s Maduro

Crude prices took a tumble on Monday after an audacious U.S. capture of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro raised the prospect of more supply coming from the major Latin American producer.

Brent oil futures for March fell 0.8% to $60.27 a barrel by 03:08 ET (08:08 GMT), while West Texas Intermediate crude futures dropped 0.9% to $56.82 a barrel.

U.S. forces captured Maduro in a weekend strike, with the Venezuelan leader now set to face drug-trafficking charges in New York. President Donald Trump said that Washington will run Venezuela until a new leader is elected, and that as part of the incursion, major U.S. oil companies will be allowed to move into the country.

Shares of oil groups, Chevron, ExxonMobil, and ConocoPhillips, all surged in extended hours trading.

Venezuela has the largest proven oil reserves in the world. But output from the country has faltered due to aging infrastructure and strict U.S. sanctions.

In a note, Warren Patterson, Head of Commodities Strategy at ING said that statements from Venezuelan Vice President Delcy Rodriguez calling for the country and the U.S. to work together suggest that a "smooth transition" may be ahead despite the recent upheaval.

This would increase the likelihood that the U.S. will lift its blockade of sanctioned oil tankers entering and leaving Venezuela, putting possible short-term downward pressure on oil prices, Patterson argued.

However, a "messier transition" could put roughly 900,000 barrels per day of oil supply from Venezuela at risk, Patterson said. Some upside risk could be in play then, although this is likely to be limited in an already "well-supplied" market, Patterson added.

2. Employment data

Headlining the economic calendar this week is the upcoming release of U.S. employment data for December.

Economists expect the U.S. to have added some 57,000 roles during the final month of 2025, down from 64,000 in November. In October, a spate of government-related spending reductions triggered the sharpest decline in nonfarm payrolls in almost five years.

The cadence of monthly jobs figures is set to normalize after a prolonged, 43-day U.S. government shutdown led to the delay of these crucial reports. The previous batch of jobs data did not include an unemployment rate for October, marking the first gap in a series that stretches back to 1948.

Federal Reserve policymakers slashed interest rates at the conclusion of each of its last three gatherings in 2025, prioritizing worries over the health of a weakening labor market above signs of sticky inflationary pressures.

The outlook for lower rates has offered some support for stocks, although Fed officials appear to be divided over the path ahead for borrowing costs in 2026.

3. ISM manufacturing, services PMIs ahead

Elsewhere, closely-monitored trackers of U.S. manufacturing and services sector activity are due to be unveiled this week.

The Institute for Supply Management’s December manufacturing purchasing managers’ index -- set to be published on Monday -- is seen coming in at 48.3, edging up from 48.2 but still within the sub-50 contraction zone.

A gauge of prices paid by businesses in the segment, a metric of inflation, is also anticipated to climb slightly.

Wednesday’s ISM services PMI, on the other hand, is tipped to inch down marginally, yet remain in expansionary territory. The sector is key for the American economy, constituting more than two-thirds of overall U.S. activity.

4. Nvidia’s Huang to speak

Also on Monday, Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang is scheduled to deliver a keynote address ahead of the hotly-awaited CES tech trade show in Las Vegas.

Nvidia has become one of the figureheads of the surge in enthusiasm around artificial intelligence which has largely underpinned stocks in recent years, with the firm’s chips serving as lynchpins in the massive data centers powering AI models.

Any comments from Huang around the future of Nvidia’s processors, or indeed the larger AI industry as a whole, will be in focus as a result.

Lisa Su, head of Nvidia-rival Advanced Micro Devices, is also slated to speak at CES.

5. Warner Bros to reject Paramount’s latest bid this week - report

Meanwhile, the ongoing saga over the fate of Warner Bros Discovery is reportedly due to see yet another twist this week.

Media reports have said that the Hollywood stalwart is likely to reject a sweetened takeover bid from Paramount Skydance, even though Paramount’s $108.4 billion hostile bid came with a personal guarantee from billionaire Larry Ellison.

Citing a person familiar with the matter, Warner’s board has not yet made a final decision, but is expected to in the coming days. Paramount has said a tie-up with Warner -- which would create an entertainment juggernaut larger even than industry titan Disney -- would face fewer regulatory hurdles.

Should the board opt to dismiss Paramount’s offer, Warner would remain in position to pursue a competing $82.7 billion cash-and-stock deal with streaming giant Netflix.

title: Stifel reiterates Buy rating on Penguin Solutions stock ahead of earnings
content:
Penguin Solutions is scheduled to release its F1Q(Nov) earnings after market close on January 6. Stifel expects the company to at least meet its quarterly estimates and suggests that FY26(Aug) revenue guidance could be tightened at or above the midpoint of the current range. With an EPS forecast of $2.1 for FY2026 and a strong analyst consensus recommendation of 1.62, market expectations remain positive despite the stock’s recent volatility.

The firm anticipates that Advanced Computing revenue will align with prior guidance of flat plus or minus 15% year-over-year. However, Memory revenue could be revised higher to reflect significant continued increases in memory pricing. This potential upside aligns with Penguin’s overall revenue growth trajectory, which showed a robust 16.91% increase in the last twelve months.

Stifel notes that while higher memory sales could be slightly dilutive to gross margins, the impact on non-GAAP EPS outlook is expected to be relatively neutral. The firm is monitoring how Penguin’s go-to-market sales strategy in Advanced Computing is improving pipeline visibility from new and existing customers.

In the Memory segment, Stifel is focused on how Penguin Solutions is navigating current supply constraints and whether these constraints might limit shipments in the near term, especially as stronger pricing is likely to drive upside to previous revenue forecasts.

In other recent news, Penguin Solutions has entered into an agreement to sell its remaining 19% interest in Zilia Technologies to Lexar Europe B.V. for $46.08 million. This transaction will result in Zilia becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Lexar Europe, and the existing quotaholders agreement will be terminated. Penguin Solutions also announced the release of its SMART 64GB DDR5-6400 ECC CSODIMM memory modules, designed for high-performance computing in challenging environments. The company is further expanding its AI portfolio by integrating NVIDIA’s latest GPUs, including the DGX B300 and RTX PRO 6000, with general availability set for early 2026. Additionally, Penguin Solutions unveiled ICE ClusterWare 13.0, which features new capabilities for AI and high-performance computing, such as patent-pending anomaly detection and auto-remediation technology. Meanwhile, Stifel has lowered its price target for Penguin Solutions to $27 from $31, citing Meta sales de-risking, but maintained a Buy rating on the stock. title: Foxconn’s Q4 revenue surges 22.07% from a year earlier on AI demand
content:
